description.description: The description of the assistant. The maximum length is 512 characters.

instructions.description: The system instructions that the assistant uses. The maximum length is 32768 characters.

tools.description: A list of tool enabled on the assistant. There can be a maximum of 128 tools per assistant. Tools can be of types `code_interpreter`, `retrieval`, or `function`.

tool_resources.description: A set of resources that are used by the assistant's tools. The resources are specific to the type of tool. For example, the `code_interpreter` tool requires a list of file IDs, while the `file_search` tool requires a list of vector store IDs.

file_ids.description: Overrides the list of file IDs made available to the `code_interpreter` tool. There can be a maximum of 20 files associated with the tool.

vector_store_ids.description: Overrides the vector store attached to this assistant. There can be a maximum of 1 vector store attached to the assistant.

metadata.description: Set of 16 key-value pairs that can be attached to an object. This can be useful for storing additional information about the object in a structured format. Keys can be a maximum of 64 characters long and values can be a maximum of 512 characters long.

temperature.description: What sampling temperature to use, between 0 and 2. Higher values like 0.8 will make the output more random, while lower values like 0.2 will make it more focused and deterministic.

top_p.description:
An alternative to sampling with temperature, called nucleus sampling, where the model considers the results of the tokens with top_p probability mass. So 0.1 means only the tokens comprising the top 10% probability mass are considered.

We generally recommend altering this or temperature but not both.

type.description: The type of tool being defined: `code_interpreter`
The type of tool being defined: `file_search`

file_search.description: Overrides for the file search tool.

max_num_results.description:
The maximum number of results the file search tool should output. The default is 20 for gpt-4* models and 5 for gpt-3.5-turbo. This number should be between 1 and 50 inclusive.

Note that the file search tool may output fewer than `max_num_results` results.

type.description: The type of tool being defined: `file_search`
The type of tool being defined: `function`

description.description: A description of what the function does, used by the model to choose when and how to call the function.

name.description: The name of the function to be called. Must be a-z, A-Z, 0-9, or contain underscores and dashes, with a maximum length of 64.

truncationObject.title: Thread Truncation Controls
truncationObject.description: Controls for how a thread will be truncated prior to the run. Use this to control the initial context window of the run.

type.description: The truncation strategy to use for the thread. The default is `auto`. If set to `last_messages`, the thread will be truncated to the n most recent messages in the thread. When set to `auto`, messages in the middle of the thread will be dropped to fit the context length of the model, `max_prompt_tokens`.

last_messages.description: The number of most recent messages from the thread when constructing the context for the run.

assistantsApiToolChoiceOption.description:
Controls which (if any) tool is called by the model.
`none` means the model will not call any tools and instead generates a message.
`auto` is the default value and means the model can pick between generating a message or calling a tool.
Specifying a particular tool like `{"type": "file_search"}` or `{"type": "function", "function": {"name": "my_function"}}` forces the model to call that tool.

oneOf.description: `none` means the model will not call a function and instead generates a message. `auto` means the model can pick between generating a message or calling a function.

assistantsNamedToolChoice.description: Specifies a tool the model should use. Use to force the model to call a specific tool.

type.description: The type of the tool. If type is `function`, the function name must be set

name.description: The name of the function to call.

runObject.title: A run on a thread
runObject.description: Represents an execution run on a [thread](/docs/api-reference/threads).

id.description: The identifier, which can be referenced in API endpoints.

object.description: The object type, which is always `thread.run`.

values.description: The run object type which is always thread.run

created_at.description: The Unix timestamp (in seconds) for when the run was created.

thread_id.description: The ID of the [thread](/docs/api-reference/threads) that was executed on as a part of this run.

assistant_id.description: The ID of the assistant used for execution of this run.

status.description: The status of the run, which can be either `queued`, `in_progress`, `requires_action`, `cancelling`, `cancelled`, `failed`, `completed`, or `expired`.

values.description: The queued state
The in_progress state
The required_action state
The cancelling state
The cancelled state
The failed state
The completed state
The expired state

required_action.description: Details on the action required to continue the run. Will be `null` if no action is required.

type.description: For now, this is always `submit_tool_outputs`.

submit_tool_outputs.description: Details on the tool outputs needed for this run to continue.

tool_calls.description: A list of the relevant tool calls.

last_error.description: The last error associated with this run. Will be `null` if there are no errors.

values.description: The server failed to respond to request due to server error
The server failed to respond to request due to rate limit exceeded

message.description: A human-readable description of the error.

expires_at.description: The Unix timestamp (in seconds) for when the run will expire.

started_at.description: The Unix timestamp (in seconds) for when the run was started.

cancelled_at.description: The Unix timestamp (in seconds) for when the run was cancelled.

failed_at.description: The Unix timestamp (in seconds) for when the run failed.

completed_at.description: The Unix timestamp (in seconds) for when the run was completed.

incomplete_details.description: Details on why the run is incomplete. Will be `null` if the run is not incomplete.

reason.description: The reason why the run is incomplete. This will point to which specific token limit was reached over the course of the run.

model.description: The model that the assistant used for this run.

instructions.description: The instructions that the assistant used for this run.

tools.description: The list of tools that the assistant used for this run.

metadata.description: Set of 16 key-value pairs that can be attached to an object. This can be useful for storing additional information about the object in a structured format. Keys can be a maximum of 64 characters long and values can be a maximum of 512 characters long.

temperature.description: The sampling temperature used for this run. If not set, defaults to 1.

top_p.description: The nucleus sampling value used for this run. If not set, defaults to 1.

max_prompt_tokens.description: The maximum number of prompt tokens specified to have been used over the course of the run.

max_completion_tokens.description: The maximum number of completion tokens specified to have been used over the course of the run.

assistant_id.description: The ID of the assistant to use to execute this run.

model.description: The ID of the Model to be used to execute this run. If a value is provided here, it will override the model associated with the assistant. If not, the model associated with the assistant will be used.

instructions.description: Override the default system message of the assistant. This is useful for modifying the behavior on a per-run basis.

additional_instructions.description: Appends additional instructions at the end of the instructions for the run. This is useful for modifying the behavior on a per-run basis without overriding other instructions.

additional_messages.description: Adds additional messages to the thread before creating the run.

tools.description: Override the tools the assistant can use for this run. This is useful for modifying the behavior on a per-run basis.

metadata.description: Set of 16 key-value pairs that can be attached to an object. This can be useful for storing additional information about the object in a structured format. Keys can be a maximum of 64 characters long and values can be a maximum of 512 characters long.

temperature.description: What sampling temperature to use, between 0 and 2. Higher values like 0.8 will make the output more random, while lower values like 0.2 will make it more focused and deterministic.

top_p.description:
An alternative to sampling with temperature, called nucleus sampling, where the model considers the results of the tokens with top_p probability mass. So 0.1 means only the tokens comprising the top 10% probability mass are considered.

We generally recommend altering this or temperature but not both.

stream.description: If `true`, returns a stream of events that happen during the Run as server-sent events, terminating when the Run enters a terminal state with a `data: [DONE]` message.

max_prompt_tokens.description: The maximum number of prompt tokens that may be used over the course of the run. The run will make a best effort to use only the number of prompt tokens specified, across multiple turns of the run. If the run exceeds the number of prompt tokens specified, the run will end with status `incomplete`. See `incomplete_details` for more info.

max_completion_tokens.description: The maximum number of completion tokens that may be used over the course of the run. The run will make a best effort to use only the number of completion tokens specified, across multiple turns of the run. If the run exceeds the number of completion tokens specified, the run will end with status `incomplete`. See `incomplete_details` for more info.

messageDeltaObject.title: Message delta object
messageDeltaObject.description: Represents a message delta i.e. any changed fields on a message during streaming.

id.description: The identifier of the message, which can be referenced in API endpoints.

object.description: The object type, which is always `thread.message.delta`.

delta.description: The delta containing the fields that have changed on the Message.

role.description: The entity that produced the message. One of `user` or `assistant`.

content.description: The content of the message in array of text and/or images.

role.description:
The role of the entity that is creating the message. Allowed values include:
- `user`: Indicates the message is sent by an actual user and should be used in most cases to represent user-generated messages.
- `assistant`: Indicates the message is generated by the assistant. Use this value to insert messages from the assistant into the conversation.

tools.description: The tools to add this file to.

attachments.description: A list of files attached to the message, and the tools they should be added to.

vectorStoreExpirationAfter.description: The expiration policy for a vector store.

vectorStoreObject.title: Vector store
vectorStoreObject.description: A vector store is a collection of processed files can be used by the `file_search` tool.

id.description: The identifier, which can be referenced in API endpoints.

object.description: The object type, which is always `vector_store`.

created_at.description: The Unix timestamp (in seconds) for when the vector store was created.

name.description: The name of the vector store.

usage_bytes.description: The total number of bytes used by the files in the vector store.

in_progress.description: The number of files that are currently being processed.

completed.description: The number of files that have been successfully processed.

failed.description: The number of files that have failed to process.

cancelled.description: The number of files that were cancelled.

status.description: The status of the vector store, which can be either `expired`, `in_progress`, or `completed`. A status of `completed` indicates that the vector store is ready for use.

expires_at.description: The Unix timestamp (in seconds) for when the vector store will expire.

last_active_at.description: The Unix timestamp (in seconds) for when the vector store was last active.

metadata.description: Set of 16 key-value pairs that can be attached to an object. This can be useful for storing additional information about the object in a structured format. Keys can be a maximum of 64 characters long and values can be a maximum of 512 characters long.

file_ids.description: A list of file IDs that the vector store should use. Useful for tools like `file_search` that can access files.

name.description: The name of the vector store.

chunking_strategy.description: The chunking strategy used to chunk the file(s). If not set, will use the `auto` strategy. Only applicable if `file_ids` is non-empty.

vectorStoreFileObject.title: Vector store files
vectorStoreFileObject.description: A list of files attached to a vector store.

id.description: The identifier, which can be referenced in API endpoints.

object.description: The object type, which is always `vector_store.file`.

usage_bytes.description: The total vector store usage in bytes. Note that this may be different from the original file size.

created_at.description: The Unix timestamp (in seconds) for when the vector store file was created.

vector_store_id.description: The ID of the vector store that the file is attached to.

status.description: The status of the vector store file, which can be either `in_progress`, `completed`, `cancelled`, or `failed`. The status `completed` indicates that the vector store file is ready for use.

last_error.description: The last error associated with this vector store file. Will be `null` if there are no errors.

message.description: A human-readable description of the error.

chunking_strategy.description: The chunking strategy used to chunk the file(s). If not set, will use the `auto` strategy. Only applicable if `file_ids` is non-empty.

otherChunkingStrategyResponseParam.title: Other Chunking Strategy
otherChunkingStrategyResponseParam.description: This is returned when the chunking strategy is unknown. Typically, this is because the file was indexed before the `chunking_strategy` concept was introduced in the API.

type.description: Always `other`.
Always `static`.

max_chunk_size_tokens.description: The maximum number of tokens in each chunk. The default value is `800`. The minimum value is `100` and the maximum value is `4096`.

chunk_overlap_tokens.description:
The number of tokens that overlap between chunks. The default value is `400`.

Note that the overlap must not exceed half of `max_chunk_size_tokens`.

autoChunkingStrategyRequestParam.title: Auto Chunking Strategy
autoChunkingStrategyRequestParam.description: The default strategy. This strategy currently uses a `max_chunk_size_tokens` of `800` and `chunk_overlap_tokens` of `400`.

type.description: Always `auto`.
Always `static`.

chunkingStrategyRequestParam.description: The chunking strategy used to chunk the file(s). If not set, will use the `auto` strategy.

file_id.description: A File ID that the vector store should use. Useful for tools like `file_search` that can access files.

assistantStreamEvent.description:
Represents an event emitted when streaming a Run.

Each event in a server-sent events stream has an `event` and `data` property:

```
event: thread.created
data: {"id": "thread_123", "object": "thread", ...}
```

We emit events whenever a new object is created, transitions to a new state, or is being
streamed in parts (deltas). For example, we emit `thread.run.created` when a new run
is created, `thread.run.completed` when a run completes, and so on. When an Assistant chooses
to create a message during a run, we emit a `thread.message.created event`, a
`thread.message.in_progress` event, many `thread.message.delta` events, and finally a
`thread.message.completed` event.

We may add additional events over time, so we recommend handling unknown events gracefully
in your code.

values.description: The thread created event

oneOf.description: Occurs when a new thread is created.
Occurs when a new run is created.
Occurs when a run moves to a `queued` status.
Occurs when a run moves to an `in_progress` status.
Occurs when a run moves to a `requires_action` status.
Occurs when a run is completed.
Occurs when a run fails.
Occurs when a run moves to a `cancelling` status.
Occurs when a run is cancelled.
Occurs when a run expires.
Occurs when a run step is created.
Occurs when a run step moves to an `in_progress` state.
Occurs when parts of a run step are being streamed.
Occurs when a run step is completed.
Occurs when a run step fails.
Occurs when a run step is cancelled.
Occurs when a run step expires.
Occurs when a message is created.
Occurs when a message moves to an `in_progress` state.
Occurs when parts of a message are being streamed.
Occurs when a message is completed.
Occurs when a message ends before it is completed.

type.description:
Specifies the event type. For a click action, this property is 
always set to `click`.

button.description: Indicates which mouse button was pressed during the click. One of `left`, `right`, `wheel`, `back`, or `forward`.

x.description: The x-coordinate where the click occurred.

y.description: The y-coordinate where the click occurred.

CodeInterpreterFileOutput.description: The output of a code interpreter tool call that is a file.

type.description: The type of the code interpreter file output. Always `files`.

mime_type.description: The MIME type of the file.

file_id.description: The ID of the file.

CodeInterpreterTextOutput.title: Code interpreter text output
CodeInterpreterTextOutput.description: The output of a code interpreter tool call that is text.

type.description: The type of the code interpreter text output. Always `logs`.

logs.description: The logs of the code interpreter tool call.

type.description: The type of the code interpreter tool. Always `code_interpreter`.

file_ids.description: The IDs of the files to run the code on.

CodeInterpreterToolCall.title: Code interpreter tool call
CodeInterpreterToolCall.description: A tool call to run code.

id.description: The unique ID of the code interpreter tool call.

type.description: The type of the code interpreter tool call. Always `code_interpreter_call`.

status.description: The status of the code interpreter tool call.

results.description: The results of the code interpreter tool call.

ComparisonFilter.title: Comparison Filter
ComparisonFilter.description: A filter used to compare a specified attribute key to a given value using a defined comparison operation.

type.description:
Specifies the comparison operator: `eq`, `ne`, `gt`, `gte`, `lt`, `lte`.
- `eq`: equals
- `ne`: not equal
- `gt`: greater than
- `gte`: greater than or equal
- `lt`: less than
- `lte`: less than or equal

key.description: The key to compare against the value.

value.description: The value to compare against the attribute key; supports string, number, or boolean types.

type.description: Type of operation: `and` or `or`.

filters.description: Array of filters to combine. Items can be `ComparisonFilter` or `CompoundFilter`.

ComputerScreenshotImage.description: A computer screenshot image used with the computer use tool.

type.description:
Specifies the event type. For a computer screenshot, this property is 
always set to `computer_screenshot`.

image_url.description: The URL of the screenshot image.

file_id.description: The identifier of an uploaded file that contains the screenshot.

ComputerTool.title: Computer use
ComputerTool.description:
A tool that controls a virtual computer. Learn more about the 
[computer tool](/docs/guides/tools-computer-use).

type.description: The type of the computer use tool. Always `computer_use_preview`.

display_width.description: The width of the computer display.

display_height.description: The height of the computer display.

environment.description: The type of computer environment to control.

ComputerToolCall.title: Computer tool call
ComputerToolCall.description:
A tool call to a computer use tool. See the 
[computer use guide](/docs/guides/tools-computer-use) for more information.

type.description: The type of the computer call. Always `computer_call`.

id.description: The unique ID of the computer call.

call_id.description: An identifier used when responding to the tool call with output.

pending_safety_checks.description: The pending safety checks for the computer call.

status.description:
The status of the item. One of `in_progress`, `completed`, or
`incomplete`. Populated when items are returned via API.

ComputerToolCallOutput.title: Computer tool call output
ComputerToolCallOutput.description: The output of a computer tool call.

type.description: The type of the computer tool call output. Always `computer_call_output`.

id.description: The ID of the computer tool call output.

call_id.description: The ID of the computer tool call that produced the output.

acknowledged_safety_checks.description:
The safety checks reported by the API that have been acknowledged by the 
developer.

status.description:
The status of the message input. One of `in_progress`, `completed`, or
`incomplete`. Populated when input items are returned via API.

id.description: The unique ID of the computer call tool output.

ComputerToolCallSafetyCheck.description: A pending safety check for the computer call.

id.description: The ID of the pending safety check.

code.description: The type of the pending safety check.

message.description: Details about the pending safety check.

Coordinate.title: Coordinate
Coordinate.description: An x/y coordinate pair, e.g. `{ x: 100, y: 200 }`.

input.description:
Text, image, or file inputs to the model, used to generate a response.

Learn more:
- [Text inputs and outputs](/docs/guides/text)
- [Image inputs](/docs/guides/images)
- [File inputs](/docs/guides/pdf-files)
- [Conversation state](/docs/guides/conversation-state)
- [Function calling](/docs/guides/function-calling)

oneOf.title: Text input
oneOf.description:
A text input to the model, equivalent to a text input with the 
`user` role.

oneOf.title: Input item list
oneOf.description:
A list of one or many input items to the model, containing 
different content types.

include.description:
Specify additional output data to include in the model response. Currently
supported values are:
- `file_search_call.results`: Include the search results of
  the file search tool call.
- `message.input_image.image_url`: Include image urls from the input message.
- `computer_call_output.output.image_url`: Include image urls from the computer call output.

parallel_tool_calls.description: Whether to allow the model to run tool calls in parallel.

store.description:
Whether to store the generated model response for later retrieval via
API.

stream.description:
If set to true, the model response data will be streamed to the client
as it is generated using [server-sent events](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Server-sent_events/Using_server-sent_events#Event_stream_format).
See the [Streaming section below](/docs/api-reference/responses-streaming)
for more information.

type.description:
Specifies the event type. For a double click action, this property is 
always set to `double_click`.

x.description: The x-coordinate where the double click occurred.

y.description: The y-coordinate where the double click occurred.

type.description:
Specifies the event type. For a drag action, this property is 
always set to `drag`.

path.description:
An array of coordinates representing the path of the drag action. Coordinates will appear as an array
of objects, eg
```
[
  { x: 100, y: 200 },
  { x: 200, y: 300 }
]
```

items.title: Drag path coordinates
items.description: A series of x/y coordinate pairs in the drag path.

EasyInputMessage.title: Input message
EasyInputMessage.description:
A message input to the model with a role indicating instruction following
hierarchy. Instructions given with the `developer` or `system` role take
precedence over instructions given with the `user` role. Messages with the
`assistant` role are presumed to have been generated by the model in previous
interactions.

role.description:
The role of the message input. One of `user`, `assistant`, `system`, or
`developer`.

content.description:
Text, image, or audio input to the model, used to generate a response.
Can also contain previous assistant responses.

oneOf.title: Text input
oneOf.description: A text input to the model.

type.description: The type of the message input. Always `message`.
The type of the file citation. Always `file_citation`.

index.description: The index of the file in the list of files.

file_id.description: The ID of the file.

type.description: The type of the file path. Always `file_path`.

file_id.description: The ID of the file.

index.description: The index of the file in the list of files.

FileSearchRanker.description: The ranker to use for the file search. If not specified will use the `auto` ranker.

FileSearchTool.title: File search
FileSearchTool.description:
A tool that searches for relevant content from uploaded files.
Learn more about the [file search tool](/docs/guides/tools-file-search).

type.description: The type of the file search tool. Always `file_search`.

vector_store_ids.description: The IDs of the vector stores to search.

max_num_results.description:
The maximum number of results to return. This number should be between 1 
and 50 inclusive.

filters.description: A filter to apply based on file attributes.

ranker.description: The ranker to use for the file search.

score_threshold.description:
The score threshold for the file search, a number between 0 and 1.
Numbers closer to 1 will attempt to return only the most relevant
results, but may return fewer results.

FileSearchToolCall.title: File search tool call
FileSearchToolCall.description:
The results of a file search tool call. See the 
[file search guide](/docs/guides/tools-file-search) for more information.

id.description: The unique ID of the file search tool call.

type.description: The type of the file search tool call. Always `file_search_call`.

status.description:
The status of the file search tool call. One of `in_progress`, 
`searching`, `incomplete` or `failed`,

queries.description: The queries used to search for files.

results.description: The results of the file search tool call.

file_id.description: The unique ID of the file.

text.description: The text that was retrieved from the file.

score.description: The relevance score of the file - a value between 0 and 1.

FunctionTool.title: Function
FunctionTool.description:
Defines a function in your own code the model can choose to call. Learn more
about [function calling](/docs/guides/function-calling).

type.description: The type of the function tool. Always `function`.

name.description: The name of the function to call.

description.description:
A description of the function. Used by the model to determine whether
or not to call the function.

parameters.description: A JSON schema object describing the parameters of the function.

strict.description: Whether to enforce strict parameter validation. Default `true`.

FunctionToolCall.title: Function tool call
FunctionToolCall.description:
A tool call to run a function. See the 
[function calling guide](/docs/guides/function-calling) for more information.

id.description: The unique ID of the function tool call.

type.description: The type of the function tool call. Always `function_call`.

call_id.description: The unique ID of the function tool call generated by the model.

name.description: The name of the function to run.

arguments.description: A JSON string of the arguments to pass to the function.

status.description:
The status of the item. One of `in_progress`, `completed`, or
`incomplete`. Populated when items are returned via API.

FunctionToolCallOutput.title: Function tool call output
FunctionToolCallOutput.description: The output of a function tool call.

id.description:
The unique ID of the function tool call output. Populated when this item
is returned via API.

type.description: The type of the function tool call output. Always `function_call_output`.

call_id.description: The unique ID of the function tool call generated by the model.

output.description: A JSON string of the output of the function tool call.

status.description:
The status of the item. One of `in_progress`, `completed`, or
`incomplete`. Populated when items are returned via API.

id.description: The unique ID of the function call tool output.

includable.description:
Specify additional output data to include in the model response. Currently
supported values are:
- `file_search_call.results`: Include the search results of
  the file search tool call.
- `message.input_image.image_url`: Include image urls from the input message.
- `computer_call_output.output.image_url`: Include image urls from the computer call output.

InputAudio.title: Audio input
InputAudio.description: An audio input to the model.

type.description: The type of the input item. Always `input_audio`.

data.description: Base64-encoded audio data.

format.description:
The format of the audio data. Currently supported formats are `mp3` and
`wav`.

InputFile.title: File input
InputFile.description: A file input to the model.

type.description: The type of the input item. Always `input_file`.

file_id.description: The ID of the file to be sent to the model.

filename.description: The name of the file to be sent to the model.

file_data.description: The content of the file to be sent to the model.

InputImage.title: Image input
InputImage.description: An image input to the model. Learn about [image inputs](/docs/guides/vision).

type.description: The type of the input item. Always `input_image`.

image_url.description:
The URL of the image to be sent to the model. A fully qualified URL or
base64 encoded image in a data URL.

file_id.description: The ID of the file to be sent to the model.

detail.description:
The detail level of the image to be sent to the model. One of `high`,
`low`, or `auto`. Defaults to `auto`.

oneOf.title: Item
oneOf.description:
An item representing part of the context for the response to be 
generated by the model. Can contain text, images, and audio inputs,
as well as previous assistant responses and tool call outputs.

InputMessage.title: Input message
InputMessage.description:
A message input to the model with a role indicating instruction following
hierarchy. Instructions given with the `developer` or `system` role take
precedence over instructions given with the `user` role.

type.description: The type of the message input. Always set to `message`.

role.description: The role of the message input. One of `user`, `system`, or `developer`.

status.description:
The status of item. One of `in_progress`, `completed`, or
`incomplete`. Populated when items are returned via API.

InputMessageContentList.title: Input item content list
InputMessageContentList.description:
A list of one or many input items to the model, containing different content 
types.

id.description: The unique ID of the message input.

InputText.title: Text input
InputText.description: A text input to the model.

type.description: The type of the input item. Always `input_text`.

text.description: The text input to the model.

Item.description: Content item used to generate a response.

ItemReference.title: Item reference
ItemReference.description: An internal identifier for an item to reference.

id.description: The ID of the item to reference.

type.description: The type of item to reference. Always `item_reference`.

ItemResource.description: Content item used to generate a response.

KeyPress.title: KeyPress
KeyPress.description: A collection of keypresses the model would like to perform.

type.description:
Specifies the event type. For a keypress action, this property is 
always set to `keypress`.

items.description: One of the keys the model is requesting to be pressed.

keys.description:
The combination of keys the model is requesting to be pressed. This is an
array of strings, each representing a key.

Metadata.description:
Set of 16 key-value pairs that can be attached to an object. This can be
useful for storing additional information about the object in a structured
format, and querying for objects via API or the dashboard. 

Keys are strings with a maximum length of 64 characters. Values are strings
with a maximum length of 512 characters.

model.description: Model used to generate the responses.

temperature.description:
What sampling temperature to use, between 0 and 2. Higher values like 0.8 will make the output more random, while lower values like 0.2 will make it more focused and deterministic.
We generally recommend altering this or `top_p` but not both.

top_p.description:
An alternative to sampling with temperature, called nucleus sampling,
where the model considers the results of the tokens with top_p probability
mass. So 0.1 means only the tokens comprising the top 10% probability mass
are considered.

We generally recommend altering this or `temperature` but not both.

user.description: A unique identifier representing your end-user, which can help OpenAI to monitor and detect abuse. [Learn more](/docs/guides/safety-best-practices#end-user-ids).

type.description:
Specifies the event type. For a move action, this property is 
always set to `move`.

OutputAudio.title: Output audio
OutputAudio.description: An audio output from the model.

type.description: The type of the output audio. Always `output_audio`.

data.description: Base64-encoded audio data from the model.

transcript.description: The transcript of the audio data from the model.

OutputMessage.title: Output message
OutputMessage.description: An output message from the model.

id.description: The unique ID of the output message.

type.description: The type of the output message. Always `message`.

role.description: The role of the output message. Always `assistant`.

content.description: The content of the output message.

status.description:
The status of the message input. One of `in_progress`, `completed`, or
`incomplete`. Populated when input items are returned via API.

OutputText.title: Output text
OutputText.description: A text output from the model.

type.description: The type of the output text. Always `output_text`.

text.description: The text output from the model.

annotations.description: The annotations of the text output.

generate_summary.description:
**o-series models only** 

A summary of the reasoning performed by the model. This can be
useful for debugging and understanding the model's reasoning process.

ResponseModalities.description:
Output types that you would like the model to generate.
Most models are capable of generating text, which is the default:

`["text"]`

The `gpt-4o-audio-preview` model can also be used to 
[generate audio](/docs/guides/audio). To request that this model generate 
both text and audio responses, you can use:

`["text", "audio"]`

ResponseModalitiesTextOnly.description:
Output types that you would like the model to generate.
Most models are capable of generating text, which is the default:

`["text"]`

This API will soon support other output modalities, including audio and images.

previous_response_id.description:
The unique ID of the previous response to the model. Use this to
create multi-turn conversations. Learn more about 
[conversation state](/docs/guides/conversation-state).

max_output_tokens.description: An upper bound for the number of tokens that can be generated for a response, including visible output tokens and [reasoning tokens](/docs/guides/reasoning).

instructions.description:
Inserts a system (or developer) message as the first item in the model's context.

When using along with `previous_response_id`, the instructions from a previous
response will be not be carried over to the next response. This makes it simple
to swap out system (or developer) messages in new responses.

text.description:
Configuration options for a text response from the model. Can be plain
text or structured JSON data. Learn more:
- [Text inputs and outputs](/docs/guides/text)
- [Structured Outputs](/docs/guides/structured-outputs)

tools.description:
An array of tools the model may call while generating a response. You 
can specify which tool to use by setting the `tool_choice` parameter.

The two categories of tools you can provide the model are:

- **Built-in tools**: Tools that are provided by OpenAI that extend the
  model's capabilities, like [web search](/docs/guides/tools-web-search)
  or [file search](/docs/guides/tools-file-search). Learn more about
  [built-in tools](/docs/guides/tools).
- **Function calls (custom tools)**: Functions that are defined by you,
  enabling the model to call your own code. Learn more about
  [function calling](/docs/guides/function-calling).

tool_choice.description:
How the model should select which tool (or tools) to use when generating
a response. See the `tools` parameter to see how to specify which tools
the model can call.

truncation.description:
The truncation strategy to use for the model response.
- `auto`: If the context of this response and previous ones exceeds
  the model's context window size, the model will truncate the 
  response to fit the context window by dropping input items in the
  middle of the conversation. 
- `disabled` (default): If a model response will exceed the context window 
  size for a model, the request will fail with a 400 error.

TextResponseFormatConfiguration.description:
An object specifying the format that the model must output.

Configuring `{ "type": "json_schema" }` enables Structured Outputs, 
which ensures the model will match your supplied JSON schema. Learn more in the 
[Structured Outputs guide](/docs/guides/structured-outputs).

The default format is `{ "type": "text" }` with no additional options.

**Not recommended for gpt-4o and newer models:**

Setting to `{ "type": "json_object" }` enables the older JSON mode, which
ensures the message the model generates is valid JSON. Using `json_schema`
is preferred for models that support it.

TextResponseFormatJsonSchema.title: JSON schema
TextResponseFormatJsonSchema.description:
JSON Schema response format. Used to generate structured JSON responses.
Learn more about [Structured Outputs](/docs/guides/structured-outputs).

type.description: The type of response format being defined. Always `json_schema`.

description.description:
A description of what the response format is for, used by the model to
determine how to respond in the format.

name.description:
The name of the response format. Must be a-z, A-Z, 0-9, or contain
underscores and dashes, with a maximum length of 64.

strict.description:
Whether to enable strict schema adherence when generating the output.
If set to true, the model will always follow the exact schema defined
in the `schema` field. Only a subset of JSON Schema is supported when
`strict` is `true`. To learn more, read the [Structured Outputs
guide](/docs/guides/structured-outputs).

ToolChoiceOptions.title: Tool choice mode
ToolChoiceOptions.description:
Controls which (if any) tool is called by the model.

`none` means the model will not call any tool and instead generates a message.

`auto` means the model can pick between generating a message or calling one or
more tools.

`required` means the model must call one or more tools.

- `computer_use_preview`

index.description: The ordered index associated with this chat completions choice.

finish_reason.description: The reason that this chat completions choice completed its generated.

message.description: The chat message for a given chat completions prompt.

ChatChoice.description:
The representation of a single prompt completion as part of an overall chat completions request.
Generally, `n` choices are generated per provided prompt with a default value of 1.
Token limits and other settings may limit the number of choices generated.

id.description: A unique identifier associated with this chat completions response.

object.description: The response object type, which is always `chat.completion`.

created.description:
The first timestamp associated with generation activity for this completions response,
represented as seconds since the beginning of the Unix epoch of 00:00 on 1 Jan 1970.

model.description: The model used for the chat completion.

choices.description:
The collection of completions choices associated with this completions response.
Generally, `n` choices are generated per provided prompt with a default value of 1.
Token limits and other settings may limit the number of choices generated.

usage.description: Usage information for tokens processed and generated as part of this completions operation.

ChatCompletions.description:
Representation of the response data from a chat completions request.
Completions support a wide variety of tasks and generate text that continues from or "completes"
provided prompt data.

id.description:
Unique identifier for the audio response. This value can be used in chat history messages instead of passing 
  the full audio object.

expires_at.description:
The Unix timestamp (in seconds) at which the audio piece expires and can't be any longer referenced by its ID in 
multi-turn conversations.

data.description: Base64 encoded audio data

format.description:
The format of the audio content. If format is not provided, it will match the format used in the
input audio request.

transcript.description: The transcript of the audio file.

ChatCompletionsAudio.description: A representation of the audio generated by the model.

ChatCompletionsModality.description: The modalities that the model is allowed to use for the chat completions response.

type.description: The type of the tool. Currently, only `function` is supported.

function.description: The function that should be called.

ChatCompletionsNamedToolChoice.description: A tool selection of a specific, named function tool that will limit chat completions to using the named function.

name.description: The name of the function that should be called.

ChatCompletionsNamedToolChoiceFunction.description: A tool selection of a specific, named function tool that will limit chat completions to using the named function.

messages.description:
The collection of context messages associated with this chat completions request.
Typical usage begins with a chat message for the System role that provides instructions for
the behavior of the assistant, followed by alternating messages between the User and
Assistant roles.

frequency_penalty.description:
A value that influences the probability of generated tokens appearing based on their cumulative
frequency in generated text.
Positive values will make tokens less likely to appear as their frequency increases and
decrease the likelihood of the model repeating the same statements verbatim.
Supported range is [-2, 2].

stream.description: A value indicating whether chat completions should be streamed for this request.

presence_penalty.description:
A value that influences the probability of generated tokens appearing based on their existing
presence in generated text.
Positive values will make tokens less likely to appear when they already exist and increase the
model's likelihood to output new topics.
Supported range is [-2, 2].

temperature.description:
The sampling temperature to use that controls the apparent creativity of generated completions.
Higher values will make output more random while lower values will make results more focused
and deterministic.
It is not recommended to modify temperature and top_p for the same completions request as the
interaction of these two settings is difficult to predict.
Supported range is [0, 1].

top_p.description:
An alternative to sampling with temperature called nucleus sampling. This value causes the
model to consider the results of tokens with the provided probability mass. As an example, a
value of 0.15 will cause only the tokens comprising the top 15% of probability mass to be
considered.
It is not recommended to modify temperature and top_p for the same completions request as the
interaction of these two settings is difficult to predict.
Supported range is [0, 1].

max_tokens.description: The maximum number of tokens to generate.

response_format.description:
An object specifying the format that the model must output.

Setting to `{ "type": "json_schema", "json_schema": {...} }` enables Structured Outputs which ensures the model will match your supplied JSON schema.

Setting to `{ "type": "json_object" }` enables JSON mode, which ensures the message the model generates is valid JSON.

**Important:** when using JSON mode, you **must** also instruct the model to produce JSON yourself via a system or user message. Without this, the model may generate an unending stream of whitespace until the generation reaches the token limit, resulting in a long-running and seemingly "stuck" request. Also note that the message content may be partially cut off if `finish_reason="length"`, which indicates the generation exceeded `max_tokens` or the conversation exceeded the max context length.

stop.description: A collection of textual sequences that will end completions generation.

tools.description:
A list of tools the model may request to call. Currently, only functions are supported as a tool. The model
may response with a function call request and provide the input arguments in JSON format for that function.

tool_choice.description: If specified, the model will configure which of the provided tools it can use for the chat completions response.

seed.description:
If specified, the system will make a best effort to sample deterministically such that repeated requests with the
same seed and parameters should return the same result. Determinism is not guaranteed.

model.description: ID of the specific AI model to use, if more than one model is available on the endpoint.

modalities.description:
The modalities that the model is allowed to use for the chat completions response. The default modality
is `text`. Indicating an unsupported modality combination results in an 422 error.

user_security_context.description:
User security context contains several parameters that describe the AI application itself, and the end user that interacts with the AI application. 
These fields assist your security operations teams to investigate and mitigate security incidents by providing a comprehensive approach to protecting your AI applications. 
[Learn more](https://aka.ms/TP4AI/Documentation/EndUserContext) about protecting AI applications using Microsoft Defender for Cloud.

ChatCompletionsOptions.description:
The configuration information for a chat completions request.
Completions support a wide variety of tasks and generate text that continues from or "completes"
provided prompt data.

type.description: The response format type to use for chat completions.

ChatCompletionsResponseFormat.description:
Represents the format that the model must output. Use this to enable JSON mode instead of the default text mode.
Note that to enable JSON mode, some AI models may also require you to instruct the model to produce JSON
via a system or user message.

type.description: Response format type: always 'json_object' for this object.

ChatCompletionsResponseFormatJsonObject.description:
A response format for Chat Completions that restricts responses to emitting valid JSON objects.
Note that to enable JSON mode, some AI models may also require you to instruct the model to produce JSON
via a system or user message.

type.description: The type of response format being defined: `json_schema`

json_schema.description: The definition of the required JSON schema in the response, and associated metadata.

ChatCompletionsResponseFormatJsonSchema.description:
A response format for Chat Completions that restricts responses to emitting valid JSON objects, with a
JSON schema specified by the caller.

name.description: The name of the response format. Must be a-z, A-Z, 0-9, or contain underscores and dashes, with a maximum length of 64.

schema.description: The definition of the JSON schema

description.description: A description of the response format, used by the AI model to determine how to generate responses in this format.

strict.description:
Whether to enable strict schema adherence when generating the output.
If set to true, the model will always follow the exact schema defined in the `schema` field. Only a subset of
JSON Schema is supported when `strict` is `true`.

ChatCompletionsResponseFormatJsonSchemaD.description: The definition of the required JSON schema in the response, and associated metadata.

type.description: Response format type: always 'text' for this object.

ChatCompletionsResponseFormatText.description: A response format for Chat Completions that emits text responses. This is the default response format.

id.description: The ID of the tool call.

type.description: The type of tool call. Currently, only `function` is supported.

function.description: The details of the function call requested by the AI model.

ChatCompletionsToolCall.description: A function tool call requested by the AI model.

ChatCompletionsToolChoicePreset.description: Represents a generic policy for how a chat completions tool may be selected.

type.description: The type of the tool. Currently, only `function` is supported.

function.description: The function definition details for the function tool.

ChatCompletionsToolDefinition.description: The definition of a chat completions tool that can call a function.

type.description: The discriminated object type: always 'image_url' for this type.

audio_url.description: An internet location, which must be accessible to the model, from which the audio may be retrieved.

ChatMessageAudioContentItem.description: A structured chat content item containing an audio reference.

url.description: The URL of the audio.

ChatMessageAudioUrl.description: An internet location from which the model may retrieve an audio.

type.description: The discriminated object type.

ChatMessageContentItem.description: An abstract representation of a structured content item within a chat message.

type.description: The discriminated object type: always 'image_url' for this type.

image_url.description: An internet location, which must be accessible to the model,from which the image may be retrieved.

ChatMessageImageContentItem.description: A structured chat content item containing an image reference.

ChatMessageImageDetailLevel.description: A representation of the possible image detail levels for image-based chat completions message content.

url.description: The URL of the image.

detail.description:
The evaluation quality setting to use, which controls relative prioritization of speed, token consumption, and
accuracy.

ChatMessageImageUrl.description: An internet location from which the model may retrieve an image.

data.description: Base64 encoded audio data

format.description: The audio format of the audio content.

ChatMessageInputAudio.description: The details of an audio chat message content part.

type.description: The discriminated object type: always 'input_audio' for this type.

format.description: The audio format of the audio reference.

ChatMessageInputAudioContentItem.description: A structured chat content item containing an audio content.

type.description: The discriminated object type: always 'text' for this type.

text.description: The content of the message.

ChatMessageTextContentItem.description: A structured chat content item containing plain text.

role.description: The chat role associated with this message, which is always 'assistant' for assistant messages.

content.description: The content of the message.

tool_calls.description:
The tool calls that must be resolved and have their outputs appended to subsequent input messages for the chat
completions request to resolve as configured.

audio.description: The audio generated by a previous response in a multi-turn conversation.

ChatRequestAssistantMessage.description: A request chat message representing response or action from the assistant.

id.description: Unique identifier for the audio response. This value corresponds to the id of a previous audio completion.

ChatRequestAudioReference.description: A reference to an audio response generated by the model.

role.description: The chat role associated with this message.

ChatRequestMessage.description: An abstract representation of a chat message as provided in a request.

role.description: The chat role associated with this message, which is always 'system' for system messages.

content.description: The contents of the system message.

ChatRequestSystemMessage.description:
A request chat message containing system instructions that influence how the model will generate a chat completions
response.

role.description: The chat role associated with this message, which is always 'tool' for tool messages.

content.description: The content of the message.

tool_call_id.description: The ID of the tool call resolved by the provided content.

ChatRequestToolMessage.description: A request chat message representing requested output from a configured tool.

role.description: The chat role associated with this message, which is always 'user' for user messages.

content.description: The contents of the user message, with available input types varying by selected model.

ChatRequestUserMessage.description: A request chat message representing user input to the assistant.

role.description: The chat role associated with the message.

content.description: The content of the message.

reasoning_content.description: The reasoning content the model used for generating the response

tool_calls.description:
The tool calls that must be resolved and have their outputs appended to subsequent input messages for the chat
completions request to resolve as configured.

audio.description: The audio generated by the model as a response to the messages if the model is configured to generate audio.

ChatResponseMessage.description: A representation of a chat message as received in a response.

ChatRole.description: A description of the intended purpose of a message within a chat completions interaction.

CompletionsFinishReason.description: Representation of the manner in which a completions response concluded.

completion_tokens.description: The number of tokens generated across all completions emissions.

prompt_tokens.description: The number of tokens in the provided prompts for the completions request.

total_tokens.description: The total number of tokens processed for the completions request and response.

completion_tokens_details.description: Breakdown of tokens used in a completion.

prompt_tokens_details.description: Breakdown of tokens used in the prompt/chat history.

CompletionsUsage.description:
Representation of the token counts processed for a completions request.
Counts consider all tokens across prompts, choices, choice alternates, best_of generations, and
other consumers.

audio_tokens.description: The number of tokens corresponding to audio input.

reasoning_tokens.description: The number of tokens corresponding to reasoning.

total_tokens.description: The total number of tokens processed for the completions request and response.

CompletionsUsageDetails.description: A breakdown of tokens used in a completion.

EmbeddingEncodingFormat.description:
Specifies the types of embeddings to generate. Compressed embeddings types like `uint8`, `int8`, `ubinary` and 
`binary`, may reduce storage costs without sacrificing the integrity of the data. Returns a 422 error if the
model doesn't support the value or parameter. Read the model's documentation to know the values supported by
the your model.

EmbeddingInputType.description: Represents the input types used for embedding search.

embedding.description:
List of embedding values for the input prompt. These represent a measurement of the
vector-based relatedness of the provided input. Or a base64 encoded string of the embedding vector.

index.description: Index of the prompt to which the EmbeddingItem corresponds.

object.description: The object type of this embeddings item. Will always be `embedding`.

EmbeddingItem.description: Representation of a single embeddings relatedness comparison.

input.description:
Input text to embed, encoded as a string or array of tokens.
To embed multiple inputs in a single request, pass an array
of strings or array of token arrays.

dimensions.description:
Optional. The number of dimensions the resulting output embeddings should have.
Passing null causes the model to use its default value.
Returns a 422 error if the model doesn't support the value or parameter.

encoding_format.description: Optional. The desired format for the returned embeddings.

input_type.description:
Optional. The type of the input.
Returns a 422 error if the model doesn't support the value or parameter.

model.description: ID of the specific AI model to use, if more than one model is available on the endpoint.

EmbeddingsOptions.description: The configuration information for an embeddings request.

id.description: Unique identifier for the embeddings result.

data.description: Embedding values for the prompts submitted in the request.

usage.description: Usage counts for tokens input using the embeddings API.

object.description: The object type of the embeddings result. Will always be `list`.

model.description: The model ID used to generate this result.

EmbeddingsResult.description:
Representation of the response data from an embeddings request.
Embeddings measure the relatedness of text strings and are commonly used for search, clustering,
recommendations, and other similar scenarios.

prompt_tokens.description: Number of tokens in the request.

total_tokens.description:
Total number of tokens transacted in this request/response. Should equal the
number of tokens in the request.

EmbeddingsUsage.description: Measurement of the amount of tokens used in this request and response.

ExtraParameters.description: Controls what happens if extra parameters, undefined by the REST API, are passed in the JSON request payload.

name.description: The name of the function to call.

arguments.description:
The arguments to call the function with, as generated by the model in JSON format.
Note that the model does not always generate valid JSON, and may hallucinate parameters
not defined by your function schema. Validate the arguments in your code before calling
your function.

FunctionCall.description: The name and arguments of a function that should be called, as generated by the model.

name.description: The name of the function to be called.

description.description:
A description of what the function does. The model will use this description when selecting the function and
interpreting its parameters.

parameters.description: The parameters the function accepts, described as a JSON Schema object.

FunctionDefinition.description: The definition of a caller-specified function that chat completions may invoke in response to matching user input.

image.description: The input image encoded in base64 string as a data URL. Example: `data:image/{format};base64,{data}`.

text.description:
Optional. The text input to feed into the model (like DINO, CLIP).
Returns a 422 error if the model doesn't support the value or parameter.

ImageEmbeddingInput.description: Represents an image with optional text.

input.description:
Input image to embed. To embed multiple inputs in a single request, pass an array.
The input must not exceed the max input tokens for the model.

dimensions.description:
Optional. The number of dimensions the resulting output embeddings should have.
Passing null causes the model to use its default value.
Returns a 422 error if the model doesn't support the value or parameter.

encoding_format.description:
Optional. The number of dimensions the resulting output embeddings should have.
Passing null causes the model to use its default value.
Returns a 422 error if the model doesn't support the value or parameter.

input_type.description:
Optional. The type of the input.
Returns a 422 error if the model doesn't support the value or parameter.

model.description: ID of the specific AI model to use, if more than one model is available on the endpoint.

ImageEmbeddingsOptions.description: The configuration information for an image embeddings request.

image.description: The image generated, encoded in base64.

seed.description: The seed that can be used to generate the image.

ImageGenerationItem.description: Representation of a single image generation.

ImageGenerationOutputFormat.description: The image generation format to use in the output.

ImageGenerationPreset.description: The preset size of the image to generate.

image.description:
The input image encoded in base64 string as a data URL.
Example: `data:image/{format};base64,{data}`."

strength.description:
The degree at which the generation process uses the image prompt as guidance. 1 indicates
a generation process that fully follows the input image. 0 a generation a process that doesn't
take into consideration the prompt image.

ImageGenerationPrompt.description: Represents an image to use for guidance during the generation.

ImageGenerationQuality.description: The quality of the image to generate.

width.description: The width of the image to generate, in pixels.

height.description: The height of the image to generate, in pixels.

ImageGenerationSize.description: The size of the image to generate.

image_generations.description: The number of image generations performed.

ImageGenerationUsage.description: Usage counts for image generation API.

prompt.description:
The prompt to use for the image geneartion. Read the model documentation to understand
which language you should use to prompt the model and get specific results.

negative_prompt.description:
Optional. The prompt to use for the negative image generation. Read the model documentation
to understand to prompt the model and get specific results. If the model doesn't support
negative prompts, a 422 error is returned.

image_prompt.description: Optional. An image to use as guidance for the image generation process.

size.description: The size of the image to generate.

output_format.description:
Optional. The format in which to generate the image.
Returns a 422 error if the model doesn't support the value or parameter.

quality.description:
Optional. The quality of the image to generate.
Returns a 422 error if the model doesn't support the value or parameter.

n.description: The number of images to generate. Defaults to 1.

model.description: ID of the specific AI model to use, if more than one model is available on the endpoint.

seed.description:
If specified, the system will make a best effort to sample deterministically such that repeated requests with the
same seed and parameters should return the same result. Determinism is not guaranteed.

ImageGenerationsOptions.description: The configuration information for an image generation request.

id.description: Unique identifier for the generation result.

created.description:
The first timestamp associated with generation activity for this completions response,
represented as seconds since the beginning of the Unix epoch of 00:00 on 1 Jan 1970.

model.description: The model used for the chat completion.

data.description: Embedding values for the prompts submitted in the request.

usage.description: Usage counts for tokens input using the embeddings API.

object.description: The object type of the image generation result. Will always be `list`.

ImageGenerationsResult.description: The result of an image generation request.

model_name.description: The name of the AI model. For example: `Phi21`

model_type.description: The type of the AI model. A Unique identifier for the profile.

model_provider_name.description: The model provider name. For example: `Microsoft`

ModelInfo.description: Represents some basic information about the AI model.

audio_tokens.description: The number of tokens corresponding to audio input.

cached_tokens.description: The total number of tokens cached.

PromptUsageDetails.description: A breakdown of tokens used in the prompt/chat history.

index.description: The ordered index associated with this chat completions choice.

finish_reason.description: The reason that this chat completions choice completed its generated.

delta.description: An update to the chat message for a given chat completions prompt.

StreamingChatChoiceUpdate.description:
Represents an update to a single prompt completion when the service is streaming updates 
using Server Sent Events (SSE).
Generally, `n` choices are generated per provided prompt with a default value of 1.
Token limits and other settings may limit the number of choices generated.

id.description: A unique identifier associated with this chat completions response.

object.description: The response object type, which is always `chat.completion`.

created.description:
The first timestamp associated with generation activity for this completions response,
represented as seconds since the beginning of the Unix epoch of 00:00 on 1 Jan 1970.

model.description: The model used for the chat completion.

choices.description:
An update to the collection of completion choices associated with this completions response.
Generally, `n` choices are generated per provided prompt with a default value of 1.
Token limits and other settings may limit the number of choices generated.

usage.description: Usage information for tokens processed and generated as part of this completions operation.

StreamingChatCompletionsUpdate.description:
Represents a response update to a chat completions request, when the service is streaming updates 
using Server Sent Events (SSE).
Completions support a wide variety of tasks and generate text that continues from or "completes"
provided prompt data.

role.description: The chat role associated with the message. If present, should always be 'assistant'

content.description: The content of the message.

reasoning_content.description: The reasoning content the model used for generating the response

tool_calls.description:
The tool calls that must be resolved and have their outputs appended to subsequent input messages for the chat
completions request to resolve as configured.

StreamingChatResponseMessageUpdate.description: A representation of a chat message update as received in a streaming response.

id.description: The ID of the tool call.

function.description: Updates to the function call requested by the AI model.

StreamingChatResponseToolCallUpdate.description: An update to the function tool call information requested by the AI model.

application_name.description: The name of the application. Sensitive personal information should not be included in this field.

end_user_id.description: This identifier is the Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) user object ID used to authenticate end-users within the generative AI application. Sensitive personal information should not be included in this field.

end_user_tenant_id.description: The Microsoft 365 tenant ID the end user belongs to. It's required when the generative AI application is multi tenant.

source_ip.description: Captures the original client's IP address, accepting both IPv4 and IPv6 formats.

UserSecurityContext.description:
User security context contains several parameters that describe the AI application itself, and the end user that interacts with the AI application. 
These fields assist your security operations teams to investigate and mitigate security incidents by providing a comprehensive approach to protecting your AI applications. 
[Learn more](https://aka.ms/TP4AI/Documentation/EndUserContext) about protecting AI applications using Microsoft Defender for Cloud.

info.title: OpenAI API
info.description:
The OpenAI REST API. Please see
https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference for more details.

tags.name: Assistants
tags.description: Build Assistants that can call models and use tools.

tags.name: Audio
tags.description: Turn audio into text or text into audio.

tags.name: Chat
tags.description:
Given a list of messages comprising a conversation, the model will
return a response.

tags.name: Completions
tags.description:
Given a prompt, the model will return one or more predicted
completions, and can also return the probabilities of alternative tokens
at each position.

tags.name: Embeddings
tags.description:
Get a vector representation of a given input that can be easily
consumed by machine learning models and algorithms.

tags.name: Evals
tags.description: Manage and run evals in the OpenAI platform.

tags.name: Fine-tuning
tags.description: Manage fine-tuning jobs to tailor a model to your specific training data.

tags.name: Batch
tags.description: Create large batches of API requests to run asynchronously.

tags.name: Files
tags.description:
Files are used to upload documents that can be used with features
like Assistants and Fine-tuning.

tags.name: Uploads
tags.description: Use Uploads to upload large files in multiple parts.

tags.name: Images
tags.description: Given a prompt and/or an input image, the model will generate a new image.

tags.name: Models
tags.description: List and describe the various models available in the API.

tags.name: Moderations
tags.description:
Given text and/or image inputs, classifies if those inputs are
potentially harmful.

tags.name: Audit Logs
tags.description: List user actions and configuration changes within this organization.

get.description: Returns a list of assistants.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: A limit on the number of objects to be returned. Limit can range between 1 and 100, and the default is 20.

parameters.name: order
parameters.description: Sort order by the `created_at` timestamp of the objects. `asc` for ascending order and `desc` for descending order.

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: A cursor for use in pagination. `after` is an object ID that defines your place in the list. For instance, if you make a list request and receive 100 objects, ending with obj_foo, your subsequent call can include after=obj_foo in order to fetch the next page of the list.

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: A cursor for use in pagination. `before` is an object ID that defines your place in the list. For instance, if you make a list request and receive 100 objects, starting with obj_foo, your subsequent call can include before=obj_foo in order to fetch the previous page of the list.

post.description: Creates and executes a batch from an uploaded file of requests

input_file_id.description:
The ID of an uploaded file that contains requests for the new batch.

See [upload file](/docs/api-reference/files/create) for how to upload a file.

Your input file must be formatted as a [JSONL file](/docs/api-reference/batch/request-input), and must be uploaded with the purpose `batch`. The file can contain up to 50,000 requests, and can be up to 200 MB in size.

endpoint.description:
The endpoint to be used for all requests in the batch. Currently
`/v1/responses`, `/v1/chat/completions`, `/v1/embeddings`,
and `/v1/completions` are supported. Note that
`/v1/embeddings` batches are also restricted to a maximum of
50,000 embedding inputs across all requests in the batch.

completion_window.description:
The time frame within which the batch should be processed.
Currently only `24h` is supported.

post.summary: Creates and executes a batch from an uploaded file of requests

get.description: List your organization's batches.

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: A cursor for use in pagination. `after` is an object ID that defines your place in the list. For instance, if you make a list request and receive 100 objects, ending with obj_foo, your subsequent call can include after=obj_foo in order to fetch the next page of the list.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: A limit on the number of objects to be returned. Limit can range between 1 and 100, and the default is 20.

get.summary: List your organization's batches.

The [Batch](/docs/api-reference/batch/object) object matching the
specified ID.

post.description:
Cancels an in-progress batch. The batch will be in status `cancelling`
for up to 10 minutes, before changing to `cancelled`, where it will have
partial results (if any) available in the output file.

parameters.name: batch_id
parameters.description: The ID of the batch to cancel.

200.description: Batch is cancelling. Returns the cancelling batch's details.

x-oaiMeta.name: Cancel batch
x-oaiMeta.returns:
The [Batch](/docs/api-reference/batch/object) object matching the
specified ID.

get.description:
List stored Chat Completions. Only Chat Completions that have been stored
with the `store` parameter set to `true` will be returned.

parameters.name: model
parameters.description: The model used to generate the Chat Completions.

parameters.name: metadata
parameters.description:
A list of metadata keys to filter the Chat Completions by. Example:

`metadata[key1]=value1&metadata[key2]=value2`

parameters.name: after
parameters.description:
Identifier for the last chat completion from the previous
pagination request.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of Chat Completions to retrieve.

parameters.name: order
parameters.description:
Sort order for Chat Completions by timestamp. Use `asc` for
ascending order or `desc` for descending order. Defaults to `asc`.

x-oaiMeta.name: List Chat Completions
x-oaiMeta.returns:
A list of [Chat Completions](/docs/api-reference/chat/list-object)
matching the specified filters.

post.description:
Creates a model response for the given chat conversation. Learn more in the
[text generation](/docs/guides/text-generation), [vision](/docs/guides/vision),
and [audio](/docs/guides/audio) guides.

Parameter support can differ depending on the model used to generate the
response, particularly for newer reasoning models. Parameters that are only
supported for reasoning models are noted below. For the current state of 
unsupported parameters in reasoning models, 
[refer to the reasoning guide](/docs/guides/reasoning).

x-oaiMeta.name: Create chat completion
x-oaiMeta.returns: Returns a [chat completion](/docs/api-reference/chat/object) object, or a streamed sequence of [chat completion chunk](/docs/api-reference/chat/streaming) objects if the request is streamed.

post.description:
Create the structure of an evaluation that can be used to test a model's performance.
An evaluation is a set of testing criteria and a datasource. After creating an evaluation, you can run it on different models and model parameters. We support several types of graders and datasources.
For more information, see the [Evals guide](/docs/guides/evals).

post.description:
Upload a file that can be used across various endpoints. Individual files can be up to 512 MB, and the size of all files uploaded by one organization can be up to 100 GB.

The Assistants API supports files up to 2 million tokens and of specific file types. See the [Assistants Tools guide](/docs/assistants/tools) for details.

The Fine-tuning API only supports `.jsonl` files. The input also has certain required formats for fine-tuning [chat](/docs/api-reference/fine-tuning/chat-input) or [completions](/docs/api-reference/fine-tuning/completions-input) models.

The Batch API only supports `.jsonl` files up to 200 MB in size. The input also has a specific required [format](/docs/api-reference/batch/request-input).

Please [contact us](https://help.openai.com/) if you need to increase these storage limits.

post.description:
Creates an intermediate [Upload](/docs/api-reference/uploads/object) object
that you can add [Parts](/docs/api-reference/uploads/part-object) to.
Currently, an Upload can accept at most 8 GB in total and expires after an
hour after you create it.

Once you complete the Upload, we will create a
[File](/docs/api-reference/files/object) object that contains all the parts
you uploaded. This File is usable in the rest of our platform as a regular
File object.

For certain `purpose` values, the correct `mime_type` must be specified.
Please refer to documentation for the
[supported MIME types for your use case](/docs/assistants/tools/file-search#supported-files).

For guidance on the proper filename extensions for each purpose, please
follow the documentation on [creating a
File](/docs/api-reference/files/create).
post.summary: Creates an intermediate Upload object that you can add Parts to.

post.description: Cancels the Upload. No Parts may be added after an Upload is cancelled.

parameters.name: upload_id
parameters.description: The ID of the Upload.

post.summary: Cancels the Upload.

post.description:
Completes the [Upload](/docs/api-reference/uploads/object).

Within the returned Upload object, there is a nested [File](/docs/api-reference/files/object) object that is ready to use in the rest of the platform.

You can specify the order of the Parts by passing in an ordered list of the Part IDs.

The number of bytes uploaded upon completion must match the number of bytes initially specified when creating the Upload object. No Parts may be added after an Upload is completed.

parameters.name: upload_id
parameters.description: The ID of the Upload.

post.description:
Adds a [Part](/docs/api-reference/uploads/part-object) to an [Upload](/docs/api-reference/uploads/object) object. A Part represents a chunk of bytes from the file you are trying to upload.

Each Part can be at most 64 MB, and you can add Parts until you hit the Upload maximum of 8 GB.

It is possible to add multiple Parts in parallel. You can decide the intended order of the Parts when you [complete the Upload](/docs/api-reference/uploads/complete).

parameters.name: upload_id
parameters.description: The ID of the Upload.
