RealtimeTranscriptionSessionCreateRespon.description:
A new Realtime transcription session configuration.

When a session is created on the server via REST API, the session object
also contains an ephemeral key. Default TTL for keys is one minute. This
property is not present when a session is updated via the WebSocket API.

client_secret.description:
Ephemeral key returned by the API. Only present when the session is
created on the server via REST API.

value.description:
Ephemeral key usable in client environments to authenticate connections
to the Realtime API. Use this in client-side environments rather than
a standard API token, which should only be used server-side.

expires_at.description:
Timestamp for when the token expires. Currently, all tokens expire
after one minute.

modalities.description:
The set of modalities the model can respond with. To disable audio,
set this to ["text"].

input_audio_format.description: The format of input audio. Options are `pcm16`, `g711_ulaw`, or `g711_alaw`.

model.description: The model to use for transcription. Can be `gpt-4o-transcribe`, `gpt-4o-mini-transcribe`, or `whisper-1`.

language.description:
The language of the input audio. Supplying the input language in
[ISO-639-1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_ISO_639-1_codes) (e.g. `en`) format
will improve accuracy and latency.

prompt.description:
An optional text to guide the model's style or continue a previous audio
segment. The [prompt](/docs/guides/speech-to-text#prompting) should match
the audio language.

turn_detection.description:
Configuration for turn detection. Can be set to `null` to turn off. Server
VAD means that the model will detect the start and end of speech based on
audio volume and respond at the end of user speech.

type.description: Type of turn detection, only `server_vad` is currently supported.

threshold.description:
Activation threshold for VAD (0.0 to 1.0), this defaults to 0.5. A
higher threshold will require louder audio to activate the model, and
thus might perform better in noisy environments.

prefix_padding_ms.description:
Amount of audio to include before the VAD detected speech (in 
milliseconds). Defaults to 300ms.

silence_duration_ms.description:
Duration of silence to detect speech stop (in milliseconds). Defaults
to 500ms. With shorter values the model will respond more quickly,
but may jump in on short pauses from the user.

description.description:
A summary of the reasoning performed by the model. This can be
useful for debugging and understanding the model's reasoning process.
One of `auto`, `concise`, or `detailed`.

generate_summary.description:
**Deprecated:** use `summary` instead.

A summary of the reasoning performed by the model. This can be
useful for debugging and understanding the model's reasoning process.
One of `auto`, `concise`, or `detailed`.

ReasoningEffort.description:
**o-series models only** 

Constrains effort on reasoning for 
[reasoning models](https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/reasoning).
Currently supported values are `low`, `medium`, and `high`. Reducing
reasoning effort can result in faster responses and fewer tokens used
on reasoning in a response.

ReasoningItem.description:
A description of the chain of thought used by a reasoning model while generating
a response.
ReasoningItem.title: Reasoning

type.description: The type of the object. Always `reasoning`.

id.description: The unique identifier of the reasoning content.

description.description: Reasoning text contents.

type.description: The type of the object. Always `summary_text`.

text.description:
A short summary of the reasoning used by the model when generating
the response.

status.description:
The status of the item. One of `in_progress`, `completed`, or
`incomplete`. Populated when items are returned via API.

id.description: Unique identifier for this Response.

object.description: The object type of this resource - always set to `response`.

status.description:
The status of the response generation. One of `completed`, `failed`,
`in_progress`, or `incomplete`.

created_at.description: Unix timestamp (in seconds) of when this Response was created.

incomplete_details.description: Details about why the response is incomplete.

reason.description: The reason why the response is incomplete.

output.description:
An array of content items generated by the model.

- The length and order of items in the `output` array is dependent  on the model's response.
- Rather than accessing the first item in the `output` array and  assuming it's an `assistant` message with the content generated by
  the model, you might consider using the `output_text` property where
  supported in SDKs.

output_text.description:
SDK-only convenience property that contains the aggregated text output
from all `output_text` items in the `output` array, if any are present.
Supported in the Python and JavaScript SDKs.

parallel_tool_calls.description: Whether to allow the model to run tool calls in parallel.

ResponseAudioDeltaEvent.description: Emitted when there is a partial audio response.

type.description: The type of the event. Always `response.audio.delta`.

delta.description: A chunk of Base64 encoded response audio bytes.

ResponseAudioDoneEvent.description: Emitted when the audio response is complete.

type.description: The type of the event. Always `response.audio.done`.

ResponseAudioTranscriptDeltaEvent.description: Emitted when there is a partial transcript of audio.

type.description: The type of the event. Always `response.audio.transcript.delta`.

delta.description: The partial transcript of the audio response.

ResponseAudioTranscriptDoneEvent.description: Emitted when the full audio transcript is completed.

type.description: The type of the event. Always `response.audio.transcript.done`.

ResponseCodeInterpreterCallCodeDeltaEven.description: Emitted when a partial code snippet is added by the code interpreter.

type.description: The type of the event. Always `response.code_interpreter_call.code.delta`.

output_index.description: The index of the output item that the code interpreter call is in progress.

delta.description: The partial code snippet added by the code interpreter.

ResponseCodeInterpreterCallCodeDoneEvent.description: Emitted when code snippet output is finalized by the code interpreter.

type.description: The type of the event. Always `response.code_interpreter_call.code.done`.

output_index.description: The index of the output item that the code interpreter call is in progress.

code.description: The final code snippet output by the code interpreter.

ResponseCodeInterpreterCallCompletedEven.description: Emitted when the code interpreter call is completed.

type.description: The type of the event. Always `response.code_interpreter_call.completed`.

output_index.description: The index of the output item that the code interpreter call is in progress.

ResponseCodeInterpreterCallInProgressEve.description: Emitted when a code interpreter call is in progress.

type.description: The type of the event. Always `response.code_interpreter_call.in_progress`.

output_index.description: The index of the output item that the code interpreter call is in progress.

ResponseCodeInterpreterCallInterpretingE.description:
Emitted when the code interpreter is actively interpreting the code
snippet.

type.description: The type of the event. Always `response.code_interpreter_call.interpreting`.

output_index.description: The index of the output item that the code interpreter call is in progress.

ResponseCompletedEvent.description: Emitted when the model response is complete.

type.description: The type of the event. Always `response.completed`.

ResponseContentPartAddedEvent.description: Emitted when a new content part is added.

type.description: The type of the event. Always `response.content_part.added`.

item_id.description: The ID of the output item that the content part was added to.

output_index.description: The index of the output item that the content part was added to.

content_index.description: The index of the content part that was added.

part.description: The content part that was added.

ResponseContentPartDoneEvent.description: Emitted when a content part is done.

type.description: The type of the event. Always `response.content_part.done`.

item_id.description: The ID of the output item that the content part was added to.

output_index.description: The index of the output item that the content part was added to.

content_index.description: The index of the content part that is done.

part.description: The content part that is done.

ResponseCreatedEvent.description: An event that is emitted when a response is created.

type.description: The type of the event. Always `response.created`.

ResponseError.description: An error object returned when the model fails to generate a Response.

message.description: A human-readable description of the error.

ResponseErrorCode.description: The error code for the response.

type.description: The type of the event. Always `error`.

message.description: The error message.

ResponseFailedEvent.description: An event that is emitted when a response fails.

type.description: The type of the event. Always `response.failed`.

ResponseFileSearchCallCompletedEvent.description: Emitted when a file search call is completed (results found).

type.description: The type of the event. Always `response.file_search_call.completed`.

output_index.description: The index of the output item that the file search call is initiated.

item_id.description: The ID of the output item that the file search call is initiated.

ResponseFileSearchCallInProgressEvent.description: Emitted when a file search call is initiated.

type.description: The type of the event. Always `response.file_search_call.in_progress`.

output_index.description: The index of the output item that the file search call is initiated.

item_id.description: The ID of the output item that the file search call is initiated.

ResponseFileSearchCallSearchingEvent.description: Emitted when a file search is currently searching.

type.description: The type of the event. Always `response.file_search_call.searching`.

output_index.description: The index of the output item that the file search call is searching.

item_id.description: The ID of the output item that the file search call is initiated.

ResponseFormatJsonObject.title: JSON object
ResponseFormatJsonObject.description:
JSON object response format. An older method of generating JSON responses.
Using `json_schema` is recommended for models that support it. Note that the
model will not generate JSON without a system or user message instructing it
to do so.

type.description: The type of response format being defined. Always `json_object`.

ResponseFormatJsonSchema.title: JSON schema
ResponseFormatJsonSchema.description:
JSON Schema response format. Used to generate structured JSON responses.
Learn more about [Structured Outputs](/docs/guides/structured-outputs).

type.description: The type of response format being defined. Always `json_schema`.

json_schema.title: JSON schema
json_schema.description: Structured Outputs configuration options, including a JSON Schema.

description.description:
A description of what the response format is for, used by the model to
determine how to respond in the format.

name.description:
The name of the response format. Must be a-z, A-Z, 0-9, or contain
underscores and dashes, with a maximum length of 64.

strict.description:
Whether to enable strict schema adherence when generating the output.
If set to true, the model will always follow the exact schema defined
in the `schema` field. Only a subset of JSON Schema is supported when
`strict` is `true`. To learn more, read the [Structured Outputs
guide](/docs/guides/structured-outputs).

ResponseFormatJsonSchemaSchema.title: JSON schema
ResponseFormatJsonSchemaSchema.description:
The schema for the response format, described as a JSON Schema object.
Learn how to build JSON schemas [here](https://json-schema.org/).

ResponseFormatText.title: Text
ResponseFormatText.description: Default response format. Used to generate text responses.

type.description: The type of response format being defined. Always `text`.

ResponseFunctionCallArgumentsDeltaEvent.description: Emitted when there is a partial function-call arguments delta.

type.description: The type of the event. Always `response.function_call_arguments.delta`.

item_id.description: The ID of the output item that the function-call arguments delta is added to.

output_index.description: The index of the output item that the function-call arguments delta is added to.

delta.description: The function-call arguments delta that is added.

ResponseFunctionCallArgumentsDoneEvent.description: Emitted when function-call arguments are finalized.

item_id.description: The ID of the item.

output_index.description: The index of the output item.

arguments.description: The function-call arguments.

ResponseInProgressEvent.description: Emitted when the response is in progress.

type.description: The type of the event. Always `response.in_progress`.

response.description: The response that is in progress.

ResponseIncompleteEvent.description: An event that is emitted when a response finishes as incomplete.

type.description: The type of the event. Always `response.incomplete`.

object.description: The type of object returned, must be `list`.

data.description: A list of items used to generate this response.

has_more.description: Whether there are more items available.

first_id.description: The ID of the first item in the list.

last_id.description: The ID of the last item in the list.

ResponseModalities.description:
Output types that you would like the model to generate.
Most models are capable of generating text, which is the default:

`["text"]`

The `gpt-4o-audio-preview` model can also be used to
[generate audio](/docs/guides/audio). To request that this model generate
both text and audio responses, you can use:

`["text", "audio"]`

ResponseOutputItemAddedEvent.description: Emitted when a new output item is added.

type.description: The type of the event. Always `response.output_item.added`.

output_index.description: The index of the output item that was added.

item.description: The output item that was added.

ResponseOutputItemDoneEvent.description: Emitted when an output item is marked done.

type.description: The type of the event. Always `response.output_item.done`.

output_index.description: The index of the output item that was marked done.

item.description: The output item that was marked done.

previous_response_id.description:
The unique ID of the previous response to the model. Use this to
create multi-turn conversations. Learn more about 
[conversation state](/docs/guides/conversation-state).

model.description:
Model ID used to generate the response, like `gpt-4o` or `o3`. OpenAI
offers a wide range of models with different capabilities, performance
characteristics, and price points. Refer to the [model guide](/docs/models)
to browse and compare available models.

max_output_tokens.description: An upper bound for the number of tokens that can be generated for a response, including visible output tokens and [reasoning tokens](/docs/guides/reasoning).

instructions.description:
Inserts a system (or developer) message as the first item in the model's context.

When using along with `previous_response_id`, the instructions from a previous
response will not be carried over to the next response. This makes it simple
to swap out system (or developer) messages in new responses.

text.description:
Configuration options for a text response from the model. Can be plain
text or structured JSON data. Learn more:
- [Text inputs and outputs](/docs/guides/text)
- [Structured Outputs](/docs/guides/structured-outputs)

tools.description:
An array of tools the model may call while generating a response. You
can specify which tool to use by setting the `tool_choice` parameter.

The two categories of tools you can provide the model are:

- **Built-in tools**: Tools that are provided by OpenAI that extend the  model's capabilities, like [web search](/docs/guides/tools-web-search)
  or [file search](/docs/guides/tools-file-search). Learn more about
  [built-in tools](/docs/guides/tools).
- **Function calls (custom tools)**: Functions that are defined by you,  enabling the model to call your own code. Learn more about
  [function calling](/docs/guides/function-calling).

tool_choice.description:
How the model should select which tool (or tools) to use when generating
a response. See the `tools` parameter to see how to specify which tools
the model can call.

truncation.description:
The truncation strategy to use for the model response.
- `auto`: If the context of this response and previous ones exceeds  the model's context window size, the model will truncate the 
  response to fit the context window by dropping input items in the
  middle of the conversation. 
- `disabled` (default): If a model response will exceed the context window  size for a model, the request will fail with a 400 error.

ResponseReasoningSummaryPartAddedEvent.description: Emitted when a new reasoning summary part is added.

type.description: The type of the event. Always `response.reasoning_summary_part.added`.

item_id.description: The ID of the item this summary part is associated with.

output_index.description: The index of the output item this summary part is associated with.

summary_index.description: The index of the summary part within the reasoning summary.

part.description: The summary part that was added.

type.description: The type of the summary part. Always `summary_text`.

text.description: The text of the summary part.

ResponseReasoningSummaryPartDoneEvent.description: Emitted when a reasoning summary part is completed.

type.description: The type of the event. Always `response.reasoning_summary_part.done`.

item_id.description: The ID of the item this summary part is associated with.

output_index.description: The index of the output item this summary part is associated with.

summary_index.description: The index of the summary part within the reasoning summary.

type.description: The type of the summary part. Always `summary_text`.

text.description: The text of the summary part.

ResponseReasoningSummaryTextDeltaEvent.description: Emitted when a delta is added to a reasoning summary text.

type.description: The type of the event. Always `response.reasoning_summary_text.delta`.

item_id.description: The ID of the item this summary text delta is associated with.

output_index.description: The index of the output item this summary text delta is associated with.

summary_index.description: The index of the summary part within the reasoning summary.

delta.description: The text delta that was added to the summary.

ResponseReasoningSummaryTextDoneEvent.description: Emitted when a reasoning summary text is completed.

type.description: The type of the event. Always `response.reasoning_summary_text.done`.

item_id.description: The ID of the item this summary text is associated with.

output_index.description: The index of the output item this summary text is associated with.

summary_index.description: The index of the summary part within the reasoning summary.

text.description: The full text of the completed reasoning summary.

ResponseRefusalDeltaEvent.description: Emitted when there is a partial refusal text.

type.description: The type of the event. Always `response.refusal.delta`.

item_id.description: The ID of the output item that the refusal text is added to.

output_index.description: The index of the output item that the refusal text is added to.

content_index.description: The index of the content part that the refusal text is added to.

delta.description: The refusal text that is added.

ResponseRefusalDoneEvent.description: Emitted when refusal text is finalized.

type.description: The type of the event. Always `response.refusal.done`.

item_id.description: The ID of the output item that the refusal text is finalized.

output_index.description: The index of the output item that the refusal text is finalized.

content_index.description: The index of the content part that the refusal text is finalized.

refusal.description: The refusal text that is finalized.

ResponseTextAnnotationDeltaEvent.description: Emitted when a text annotation is added.

type.description: The type of the event. Always `response.output_text.annotation.added`.

item_id.description: The ID of the output item that the text annotation was added to.

output_index.description: The index of the output item that the text annotation was added to.

content_index.description: The index of the content part that the text annotation was added to.

annotation_index.description: The index of the annotation that was added.

ResponseTextDeltaEvent.description: Emitted when there is an additional text delta.

type.description: The type of the event. Always `response.output_text.delta`.

item_id.description: The ID of the output item that the text delta was added to.

output_index.description: The index of the output item that the text delta was added to.

content_index.description: The index of the content part that the text delta was added to.

delta.description: The text delta that was added.

ResponseTextDoneEvent.description: Emitted when text content is finalized.

type.description: The type of the event. Always `response.output_text.done`.

item_id.description: The ID of the output item that the text content is finalized.

output_index.description: The index of the output item that the text content is finalized.

content_index.description: The index of the content part that the text content is finalized.

text.description: The text content that is finalized.

ResponseUsage.description:
Represents token usage details including input tokens, output tokens,
a breakdown of output tokens, and the total tokens used.

input_tokens_details.description: A detailed breakdown of the input tokens.

cached_tokens.description:
The number of tokens that were retrieved from the cache. 
[More on prompt caching](/docs/guides/prompt-caching).

output_tokens_details.description: A detailed breakdown of the output tokens.

total_tokens.description: The total number of tokens used.

ResponseWebSearchCallCompletedEvent.description: Emitted when a web search call is completed.

type.description: The type of the event. Always `response.web_search_call.completed`.

output_index.description: The index of the output item that the web search call is associated with.

item_id.description: Unique ID for the output item associated with the web search call.

ResponseWebSearchCallInProgressEvent.description: Emitted when a web search call is initiated.

type.description: The type of the event. Always `response.web_search_call.in_progress`.

output_index.description: The index of the output item that the web search call is associated with.

item_id.description: Unique ID for the output item associated with the web search call.

ResponseWebSearchCallSearchingEvent.description: Emitted when a web search call is executing.

type.description: The type of the event. Always `response.web_search_call.searching`.

output_index.description: The index of the output item that the web search call is associated with.

item_id.description: Unique ID for the output item associated with the web search call.

RunCompletionUsage.description:
Usage statistics related to the run. This value will be `null` if
the run is not in a terminal state (i.e. `in_progress`, `queued`, etc.).

completion_tokens.description: Number of completion tokens used over the course of the run.

prompt_tokens.description: Number of prompt tokens used over the course of the run.

total_tokens.description: Total number of tokens used (prompt + completion).

RunObject.title: A run on a thread
RunObject.description: Represents an execution run on a [thread](/docs/api-reference/threads).

id.description: The identifier, which can be referenced in API endpoints.

object.description: The object type, which is always `thread.run`.

created_at.description: The Unix timestamp (in seconds) for when the run was created.

thread_id.description:
The ID of the [thread](/docs/api-reference/threads) that was
executed on as a part of this run.

assistant_id.description:
The ID of the [assistant](/docs/api-reference/assistants) used for
execution of this run.

status.description:
The status of the run, which can be either `queued`, `in_progress`,
`requires_action`, `cancelling`, `cancelled`, `failed`, `completed`,
`incomplete`, or `expired`.

required_action.description:
Details on the action required to continue the run. Will be `null`
if no action is required.

type.description: For now, this is always `submit_tool_outputs`.

submit_tool_outputs.description: Details on the tool outputs needed for this run to continue.

tool_calls.description: A list of the relevant tool calls.

last_error.description:
The last error associated with this run. Will be `null` if there
are no errors.

message.description: A human-readable description of the error.

expires_at.description: The Unix timestamp (in seconds) for when the run will expire.

started_at.description: The Unix timestamp (in seconds) for when the run was started.

cancelled_at.description: The Unix timestamp (in seconds) for when the run was cancelled.

failed_at.description: The Unix timestamp (in seconds) for when the run failed.

completed_at.description: The Unix timestamp (in seconds) for when the run was completed.

incomplete_details.description:
Details on why the run is incomplete. Will be `null` if the run is
not incomplete.

reason.description:
The reason why the run is incomplete. This will point to which
specific token limit was reached over the course of the run.

model.description:
The model that the [assistant](/docs/api-reference/assistants) used
for this run.

instructions.description:
The instructions that the
[assistant](/docs/api-reference/assistants) used for this run.

tools.description:
The list of tools that the
[assistant](/docs/api-reference/assistants) used for this run.

temperature.description:
The sampling temperature used for this run. If not set, defaults to
1.

top_p.description:
The nucleus sampling value used for this run. If not set, defaults
to 1.

max_prompt_tokens.description: The maximum number of prompt tokens specified to have been used over the course of the run.

max_completion_tokens.description: The maximum number of completion tokens specified to have been used over the course of the run.

RunStepCompletionUsage.description:
Usage statistics related to the run step. This value will be `null`
while the run step's status is `in_progress`.

completion_tokens.description: Number of completion tokens used over the course of the run step.

prompt_tokens.description: Number of prompt tokens used over the course of the run step.

total_tokens.description: Total number of tokens used (prompt + completion).

RunStepDeltaObject.title: Run step delta object
RunStepDeltaObject.description: Represents a run step delta i.e. any changed fields on a run step during streaming.

id.description:
The identifier of the run step, which can be referenced in API
endpoints.

object.description: The object type, which is always `thread.run.step.delta`.

delta.description: The delta containing the fields that have changed on the run step.

step_details.description: The details of the run step.

RunStepDeltaStepDetailsMessageCreationOb.title: Message creation
RunStepDeltaStepDetailsMessageCreationOb.description: Details of the message creation by the run step.

type.description: Always `message_creation`.

message_id.description: The ID of the message that was created by this run step.

RunStepDeltaStepDetailsToolCallsCodeObje.title: Code interpreter tool call
RunStepDeltaStepDetailsToolCallsCodeObje.description: Details of the Code Interpreter tool call the run step was involved in.

index.description: The index of the tool call in the tool calls array.

id.description: The ID of the tool call.

type.description:
The type of tool call. This is always going to be
`code_interpreter` for this type of tool call.

code_interpreter.description: The Code Interpreter tool call definition.

input.description: The input to the Code Interpreter tool call.

outputs.description:
The outputs from the Code Interpreter tool call. Code Interpreter
can output one or more items, including text (`logs`) or images
(`image`). Each of these are represented by a different object
type.

index.description: The index of the output in the outputs array.

type.description: Always `image`.

file_id.description: The [file](/docs/api-reference/files) ID of the image.

RunStepDeltaStepDetailsToolCallsCodeOutp.title: Code interpreter log output
RunStepDeltaStepDetailsToolCallsCodeOutp.description: Text output from the Code Interpreter tool call as part of a run step.

index.description: The index of the output in the outputs array.

type.description: Always `logs`.

logs.description: The text output from the Code Interpreter tool call.

index.description: The index of the tool call in the tool calls array.

id.description: The ID of the tool call object.

type.description:
The type of tool call. This is always going to be `file_search` for
this type of tool call.

file_search.description: For now, this is always going to be an empty object.

index.description: The index of the tool call in the tool calls array.

id.description: The ID of the tool call object.

type.description:
The type of tool call. This is always going to be `function` for
this type of tool call.

function.description: The definition of the function that was called.

name.description: The name of the function.

arguments.description: The arguments passed to the function.

output.description:
The output of the function. This will be `null` if the outputs have
not been [submitted](/docs/api-reference/runs/submitToolOutputs)
yet.

type.description: Always `tool_calls`.

tool_calls.description: An array of tool calls the run step was involved in. These can be associated with one of three types of tools: `code_interpreter`, `file_search`, or `function`.

RunStepDetailsMessageCreationObject.title: Message creation
RunStepDetailsMessageCreationObject.description: Details of the message creation by the run step.

type.description: Always `message_creation`.

message_id.description: The ID of the message that was created by this run step.

RunStepDetailsToolCallsCodeObject.title: Code Interpreter tool call
RunStepDetailsToolCallsCodeObject.description: Details of the Code Interpreter tool call the run step was involved in.

id.description: The ID of the tool call.

type.description:
The type of tool call. This is always going to be
`code_interpreter` for this type of tool call.

code_interpreter.description: The Code Interpreter tool call definition.

input.description: The input to the Code Interpreter tool call.

outputs.description:
The outputs from the Code Interpreter tool call. Code Interpreter
can output one or more items, including text (`logs`) or images
(`image`). Each of these are represented by a different object
type.

type.description: Always `image`.

file_id.description: The [file](/docs/api-reference/files) ID of the image.

RunStepDetailsToolCallsCodeOutputLogsObj.title: Code Interpreter log output
RunStepDetailsToolCallsCodeOutputLogsObj.description: Text output from the Code Interpreter tool call as part of a run step.

type.description: Always `logs`.

logs.description: The text output from the Code Interpreter tool call.

id.description: The ID of the tool call object.

type.description:
The type of tool call. This is always going to be `file_search` for
this type of tool call.

file_search.description: For now, this is always going to be an empty object.

results.description: The results of the file search.

RunStepDetailsToolCallsFileSearchRanking.title: File search tool call ranking options
RunStepDetailsToolCallsFileSearchRanking.description: The ranking options for the file search.

score_threshold.description:
The score threshold for the file search. All values must be a
floating point number between 0 and 1.

RunStepDetailsToolCallsFileSearchResultO.title: File search tool call result
RunStepDetailsToolCallsFileSearchResultO.description: A result instance of the file search.

file_id.description: The ID of the file that result was found in.

file_name.description: The name of the file that result was found in.

score.description:
The score of the result. All values must be a floating point number
between 0 and 1.

content.description:
The content of the result that was found. The content is only
included if requested via the include query parameter.

type.description: The type of the content.

text.description: The text content of the file.

id.description: The ID of the tool call object.

type.description:
The type of tool call. This is always going to be `function` for
this type of tool call.

function.description: The definition of the function that was called.

name.description: The name of the function.

arguments.description: The arguments passed to the function.

output.description:
The output of the function. This will be `null` if the outputs have
not been [submitted](/docs/api-reference/runs/submitToolOutputs)
yet.

type.description: Always `tool_calls`.

tool_calls.description: An array of tool calls the run step was involved in. These can be associated with one of three types of tools: `code_interpreter`, `file_search`, or `function`.

RunStepObject.title: Run steps
RunStepObject.description: Represents a step in execution of a run.

id.description:
The identifier of the run step, which can be referenced in API
endpoints.

object.description: The object type, which is always `thread.run.step`.

created_at.description: The Unix timestamp (in seconds) for when the run step was created.

assistant_id.description:
The ID of the [assistant](/docs/api-reference/assistants)
associated with the run step.

thread_id.description: The ID of the [thread](/docs/api-reference/threads) that was run.

run_id.description:
The ID of the [run](/docs/api-reference/runs) that this run step is
a part of.

type.description:
The type of run step, which can be either `message_creation` or
`tool_calls`.

status.description:
The status of the run step, which can be either `in_progress`,
`cancelled`, `failed`, `completed`, or `expired`.

step_details.description: The details of the run step.

last_error.description:
The last error associated with this run step. Will be `null` if
there are no errors.

message.description: A human-readable description of the error.

expired_at.description:
The Unix timestamp (in seconds) for when the run step expired. A
step is considered expired if the parent run is expired.

cancelled_at.description: The Unix timestamp (in seconds) for when the run step was cancelled.

failed_at.description: The Unix timestamp (in seconds) for when the run step failed.

completed_at.description: The Unix timestamp (in seconds) for when the run step completed.

oneOf.description:
Occurs when a [run step](/docs/api-reference/run-steps/step-object)
is created.

Occurs when a [run step](/docs/api-reference/run-steps/step-object)
moves to an `in_progress` state.
Occurs when parts of a [run step](/docs/api-reference/run-steps/step-object) are being streamed.

Occurs when a [run step](/docs/api-reference/run-steps/step-object)
is completed.

Occurs when a [run step](/docs/api-reference/run-steps/step-object)
fails.

Occurs when a [run step](/docs/api-reference/run-steps/step-object)
is cancelled.

Occurs when a [run step](/docs/api-reference/run-steps/step-object)
expires.
Occurs when a new [run](/docs/api-reference/runs/object) is created.

Occurs when a [run](/docs/api-reference/runs/object) moves to a
`queued` status.

Occurs when a [run](/docs/api-reference/runs/object) moves to an
`in_progress` status.

Occurs when a [run](/docs/api-reference/runs/object) moves to a
`requires_action` status.
Occurs when a [run](/docs/api-reference/runs/object) is completed.

Occurs when a [run](/docs/api-reference/runs/object) ends with
status `incomplete`.
Occurs when a [run](/docs/api-reference/runs/object) fails.

Occurs when a [run](/docs/api-reference/runs/object) moves to a
`cancelling` status.
Occurs when a [run](/docs/api-reference/runs/object) is cancelled.
Occurs when a [run](/docs/api-reference/runs/object) expires.

id.description:
The ID of the tool call. This ID must be referenced when you submit
the tool outputs in using the [Submit tool outputs to
run](/docs/api-reference/runs/submitToolOutputs) endpoint.

type.description:
The type of tool call the output is required for. For now, this is
always `function`.

name.description: The name of the function.

arguments.description: The arguments that the model expects you to pass to the function.

type.description:
Specifies the event type. For a screenshot action, this property is 
always set to `screenshot`.

Specifies the event type. For a scroll action, this property is 
always set to `scroll`.

x.description: The x-coordinate where the scroll occurred.

y.description: The y-coordinate where the scroll occurred.

ServiceTier.description:
Specifies the latency tier to use for processing the request. This parameter is relevant for customers subscribed to the scale tier service:  - If set to 'auto', and the Project is Scale tier enabled, the system
    will utilize scale tier credits until they are exhausted.
  - If set to 'auto', and the Project is not Scale tier enabled, the request will be processed using the default service tier with a lower uptime SLA and no latency guarentee.
  - If set to 'default', the request will be processed using the default service tier with a lower uptime SLA and no latency guarentee.
  - If set to 'flex', the request will be processed with the Flex Processing service tier. [Learn more](/docs/guides/flex-processing).
  - When not set, the default behavior is 'auto'.

  When this parameter is set, the response body will include the `service_tier` utilized.

max_chunk_size_tokens.description:
The maximum number of tokens in each chunk. The default value is
`800`. The minimum value is `100` and the maximum value is `4096`.

chunk_overlap_tokens.description:
The number of tokens that overlap between chunks. The default value is `400`.

Note that the overlap must not exceed half of `max_chunk_size_tokens`.

StaticChunkingStrategyRequestParam.title: Static Chunking Strategy
StaticChunkingStrategyRequestParam.description:
Customize your own chunking strategy by setting chunk size and
chunk overlap.

type.description: Always `static`.
Always `static`.

StopConfiguration.description:
Not supported with latest reasoning models `o3` and `o4-mini`.

Up to 4 sequences where the API will stop generating further tokens. The
returned text will not contain the stop sequence.

tool_outputs.description: A list of tools for which the outputs are being submitted.

tool_call_id.description:
The ID of the tool call in the `required_action` object within the
run object the output is being submitted for.

output.description: The output of the tool call to be submitted to continue the run.

stream.description: If `true`, returns a stream of events that happen during the Run as server-sent events, terminating when the Run enters a terminal state with a `data: [DONE]` message.

TextResponseFormatConfiguration.description:
An object specifying the format that the model must output.

Configuring `{ "type": "json_schema" }` enables Structured Outputs,
which ensures the model will match your supplied JSON schema. Learn more in the
[Structured Outputs guide](/docs/guides/structured-outputs).

The default format is `{ "type": "text" }` with no additional options.

**Not recommended for gpt-4o and newer models:**

Setting to `{ "type": "json_object" }` enables the older JSON mode, which
ensures the message the model generates is valid JSON. Using `json_schema`
is preferred for models that support it.

TextResponseFormatJsonSchema.title: JSON schema
TextResponseFormatJsonSchema.description:
JSON Schema response format. Used to generate structured JSON responses.
Learn more about [Structured Outputs](/docs/guides/structured-outputs).

type.description: The type of response format being defined. Always `json_schema`.

description.description:
A description of what the response format is for, used by the model to
determine how to respond in the format.

name.description:
The name of the response format. Must be a-z, A-Z, 0-9, or contain
underscores and dashes, with a maximum length of 64.

strict.description:
Whether to enable strict schema adherence when generating the output.
If set to true, the model will always follow the exact schema defined
in the `schema` field. Only a subset of JSON Schema is supported when
`strict` is `true`. To learn more, read the [Structured Outputs
guide](/docs/guides/structured-outputs).

id.description: The identifier, which can be referenced in API endpoints.

object.description: The object type, which is always `thread`.

created_at.description: The Unix timestamp (in seconds) for when the thread was created.

tool_resources.description: A set of resources that are made available to the assistant's tools in this thread. The resources are specific to the type of tool. For example, the `code_interpreter` tool requires a list of file IDs, while the `file_search` tool requires a list of vector store IDs.

file_ids.description: A list of [file](/docs/api-reference/files) IDs made available to the `code_interpreter` tool. There can be a maximum of 20 files associated with the tool.

vector_store_ids.description: The [vector store](/docs/api-reference/vector-stores/object) attached to this thread. There can be a maximum of 1 vector store attached to the thread.

enabled.description: Whether to enable input audio transcription.

oneOf.description:
Occurs when a new [thread](/docs/api-reference/threads/object) is
created.

ToolChoiceFunction.title: Function tool
ToolChoiceFunction.description: Use this option to force the model to call a specific function.

type.description: For function calling, the type is always `function`.

name.description: The name of the function to call.

ToolChoiceOptions.title: Tool choice mode
ToolChoiceOptions.description:
Controls which (if any) tool is called by the model.

`none` means the model will not call any tool and instead generates a message.

`auto` means the model can pick between generating a message or calling one or
more tools.

`required` means the model must call one or more tools.

ToolChoiceTypes.title: Hosted tool
ToolChoiceTypes.description:
Indicates that the model should use a built-in tool to generate a response.
[Learn more about built-in tools](/docs/guides/tools).

type.description:
The type of hosted tool the model should to use. Learn more about
[built-in tools](/docs/guides/tools).

Allowed values are:
- `file_search`
- `web_search_preview`
- `computer_use_preview`

TranscriptTextDeltaEvent.description:
Emitted when there is an additional text delta. This is also the
first event emitted when the transcription starts. Only emitted when you
[create a transcription](/docs/api-reference/audio/create-transcription)
with the `Stream` parameter set to `true`.

type.description: The type of the event. Always `transcript.text.delta`.

delta.description: The text delta that was additionally transcribed.

logprobs.description: The log probabilities of the delta. Only included if you [create a transcription](/docs/api-reference/audio/create-transcription) with the `include[]` parameter set to `logprobs`.

token.description: The token that was used to generate the log probability.

logprob.description: The log probability of the token.

bytes.description: The bytes that were used to generate the log probability.

TranscriptTextDoneEvent.description:
Emitted when the transcription is complete. Contains the complete
transcription text. Only emitted when you [create a
transcription](/docs/api-reference/audio/create-transcription) with the
`Stream` parameter set to `true`.

type.description: The type of the event. Always `transcript.text.done`.

text.description: The text that was transcribed.

logprobs.description: The log probabilities of the individual tokens in the transcription. Only included if you [create a transcription](/docs/api-reference/audio/create-transcription) with the `include[]` parameter set to `logprobs`.

token.description: The token that was used to generate the log probability.

logprob.description: The log probability of the token.

bytes.description: The bytes that were used to generate the log probability.

id.description: Unique identifier of the segment.

start.description: Start time of the segment in seconds.

end.description: End time of the segment in seconds.

text.description: Text content of the segment.

tokens.description: Array of token IDs for the text content.

temperature.description: Temperature parameter used for generating the segment.

avg_logprob.description:
Average logprob of the segment. If the value is lower than -1,
consider the logprobs failed.

compression_ratio.description:
Compression ratio of the segment. If the value is greater than 2.4,
consider the compression failed.

no_speech_prob.description:
Probability of no speech in the segment. If the value is higher
than 1.0 and the `avg_logprob` is below -1, consider this segment
silent.

word.description: The text content of the word.

start.description: Start time of the word in seconds.

end.description: End time of the word in seconds.

TruncationObject.title: Thread Truncation Controls
TruncationObject.description:
Controls for how a thread will be truncated prior to the run. Use
this to control the intial context window of the run.

type.description:
The truncation strategy to use for the thread. The default is
`auto`. If set to `last_messages`, the thread will be truncated to
the n most recent messages in the thread. When set to `auto`,
messages in the middle of the thread will be dropped to fit the
context length of the model, `max_prompt_tokens`.

last_messages.description:
The number of most recent messages from the thread when
constructing the context for the run.

Type.title: Type
Type.description: An action to type in text.

type.description:
Specifies the event type. For a type action, this property is 
always set to `type`.

text.description: The text to type.

VectorStoreFileAttributes.description:
Set of 16 key-value pairs that can be attached to an object. This can be
useful for storing additional information about the object in a structured
format, and querying for objects via API or the dashboard. Keys are strings
with a maximum length of 64 characters. Values are strings with a maximum

Numbers closer to 1 will attempt to return only the most relevant
results, but may return fewer results.

type.description: The type of the file search tool. Always `file_search`.

vector_store_ids.description: The IDs of the vector stores to search.

max_num_results.description:
The maximum number of results to return. This number should be
between 1 and 50 inclusive.

anyOf.description: A filter to apply.

FileSearchTool.title: File search
FileSearchTool.description:
A tool that searches for relevant content from uploaded files.
Learn more about the [file search
tool](https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/tools-file-search).

type.description: The type of the function tool. Always `function`.

name.description: The name of the function to call.

anyOf.description:
A description of the function. Used by the model to determine
whether or not to call the function.
A JSON schema object describing the parameters of the function.
Whether to enforce strict parameter validation. Default `true`.

FunctionTool.title: Function
FunctionTool.description:
Defines a function in your own code the model can choose to call.
Learn more about [function
calling](https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/function-calling).

type.description: The type of location approximation. Always `approximate`.

anyOf.description:
The two-letter [ISO country
code](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_3166-1) of the user,
e.g. `US`.
Free text input for the region of the user, e.g. `California`.
Free text input for the city of the user, e.g. `San Francisco`.

The [IANA
timezone](https://timeapi.io/documentation/iana-timezones) of
the user, e.g. `America/Los_Angeles`.

type.description:
The type of the web search tool. One of `web_search_preview` or
`web_search_preview_2025_03_11`.

anyOf.description: The user's location.

search_context_size.description:
High level guidance for the amount of context window space to use
for the search. One of `low`, `medium`, or `high`. `medium` is the
default.

WebSearchPreviewTool.title: Web search preview
WebSearchPreviewTool.description:
This tool searches the web for relevant results to use in a
response. Learn more about the [web search
tool](https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/tools-web-search).

type.description: The type of the computer use tool. Always `computer_use_preview`.

environment.description: The type of computer environment to control.

display_width.description: The width of the computer display.

display_height.description: The height of the computer display.

ComputerUsePreviewTool.title: Computer use preview
ComputerUsePreviewTool.description:
A tool that controls a virtual computer. Learn more about the
[computer
tool](https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/tools-computer-use).

type.description: The type of the file citation. Always `file_citation`.

file_id.description: The ID of the file.

index.description: The index of the file in the list of files.

type.description: The type of the URL citation. Always `url_citation`.

url.description: The URL of the web resource.

start_index.description: The index of the first character of the URL citation in the message.

end_index.description: The index of the last character of the URL citation in the message.

title.description: The title of the web resource.

UrlCitationBody.title: URL citation
UrlCitationBody.description: A citation for a web resource used to generate a model response.

type.description: The type of the output text. Always `output_text`.

text.description: The text output from the model.

annotations.description: The annotations of the text output.

OutputTextContent.title: Output text
OutputTextContent.description: A text output from the model.

type.description: The type of the refusal. Always `refusal`.

refusal.description: The refusal explanationfrom the model.

id.description: The ID of the pending safety check.

anyOf.description: The type of the pending safety check.
Details about the pending safety check.

ComputerCallSafetyCheckParam.description: A pending safety check for the computer call.

anyOf.description: The ID of the computer tool call output.

call_id.description: The ID of the computer tool call that produced the output.

type.description:
The type of the computer tool call output. Always
`computer_call_output`.

anyOf.description:
The safety checks reported by the API that have been acknowledged
by the developer.

The status of the message input. One of `in_progress`, `completed`,
or `incomplete`. Populated when input items are returned via
API.

ComputerCallOutputItemParam.title: Computer tool call output
ComputerCallOutputItemParam.description: The output of a computer tool call.

anyOf.description:
The unique ID of the function tool call output. Populated when this
item is returned via API.

call_id.description: The unique ID of the function tool call generated by the model.

type.description:
The type of the function tool call output. Always
`function_call_output`.

output.description: A JSON string of the output of the function tool call.

anyOf.description:
The status of the item. One of `in_progress`, `completed`, or
`incomplete`. Populated when items are returned via API.

FunctionCallOutputItemParam.title: Function tool call output
FunctionCallOutputItemParam.description: The output of a function tool call.

anyOf.description: The type of item to reference. Always `item_reference`.

id.description: The ID of the item to reference.

ItemReferenceParam.title: Item reference
ItemReferenceParam.description: An internal identifier for an item to reference.

groups.title: Responses
groups.description:
OpenAI's most advanced interface for generating model responses. Supports
text and image inputs, and text outputs. Create stateful interactions
with the model, using the output of previous responses as input. Extend
the model's capabilities with built-in tools for file search, web search,
computer use, and more. Allow the model access to external systems and data
using function calling.

Related guides:
- [Quickstart](/docs/quickstart?api-mode=responses)
- [Text inputs and outputs](/docs/guides/text?api-mode=responses)
- [Image inputs](/docs/guides/images?api-mode=responses)
- [Structured Outputs](/docs/guides/structured-outputs?api-mode=responses)
- [Function calling](/docs/guides/function-calling?api-mode=responses)
- [Conversation state](/docs/guides/conversation-state?api-mode=responses)
- [Extend the models with tools](/docs/guides/tools?api-mode=responses)

groups.title: Streaming
groups.description:
When you [create a Response](/docs/api-reference/responses/create) with
`stream` set to `true`, the server will emit server-sent events to the
client as the Response is generated. This section contains the events that
are emitted by the server.

[Learn more about streaming responses](/docs/guides/streaming-responses?api-mode=responses).

groups.title: Chat Completions
groups.description:
The Chat Completions API endpoint will generate a model response from a
list of messages comprising a conversation.

Related guides:
- [Quickstart](/docs/quickstart?api-mode=chat)
- [Text inputs and outputs](/docs/guides/text?api-mode=chat)
- [Image inputs](/docs/guides/images?api-mode=chat)
- [Audio inputs and outputs](/docs/guides/audio?api-mode=chat)
- [Structured Outputs](/docs/guides/structured-outputs?api-mode=chat)
- [Function calling](/docs/guides/function-calling?api-mode=chat)
- [Conversation state](/docs/guides/conversation-state?api-mode=chat)

**Starting a new project?** We recommend trying [Responses](/docs/api-reference/responses)
to take advantage of the latest OpenAI platform features. Compare
[Chat Completions with Responses](/docs/guides/responses-vs-chat-completions?api-mode=responses).

groups.title: Streaming
groups.description:
Stream Chat Completions in real time. Receive chunks of completions
returned from the model using server-sent events.
[Learn more](/docs/guides/streaming-responses?api-mode=chat).

groups.title: Realtime
groups.description:
Communicate with a GPT-4o class model in real time using WebRTC or
WebSockets. Supports text and audio inputs and ouputs, along with audio
transcriptions.
[Learn more about the Realtime API](/docs/guides/realtime).

groups.title: Session tokens
groups.description:
REST API endpoint to generate ephemeral session tokens for use in client-side
applications.

groups.title: Client events
groups.description: These are events that the OpenAI Realtime WebSocket server will accept from the client.

groups.title: Server events
groups.description: These are events emitted from the OpenAI Realtime WebSocket server to the client.

groups.title: Audio
groups.description:
Learn how to turn audio into text or text into audio.

Related guide: [Speech to text](/docs/guides/speech-to-text)

groups.title: Images
groups.description:
Given a prompt and/or an input image, the model will generate a new image.
Related guide: [Image generation](/docs/guides/images)

groups.title: Embeddings
groups.description:
Get a vector representation of a given input that can be easily consumed by machine learning models and algorithms.
Related guide: [Embeddings](/docs/guides/embeddings)

groups.title: Evals
groups.description:
Create, manage, and run evals in the OpenAI platform.
Related guide: [Evals](/docs/guides/evals)

groups.title: Fine-tuning
groups.description:
Manage fine-tuning jobs to tailor a model to your specific training data.
Related guide: [Fine-tune models](/docs/guides/fine-tuning)

groups.title: Batch
groups.description:
Create large batches of API requests for asynchronous processing. The Batch API returns completions within 24 hours for a 50% discount.
Related guide: [Batch](/docs/guides/batch)

groups.title: Files
groups.description: Files are used to upload documents that can be used with features like [Assistants](/docs/api-reference/assistants), [Fine-tuning](/docs/api-reference/fine-tuning), and [Batch API](/docs/guides/batch).

groups.title: Uploads
groups.description: Allows you to upload large files in multiple parts.

groups.title: Models
groups.description: List and describe the various models available in the API. You can refer to the [Models](/docs/models) documentation to understand what models are available and the differences between them.

groups.title: Moderations
groups.description:
Given text and/or image inputs, classifies if those inputs are potentially harmful across several categories.
Related guide: [Moderations](/docs/guides/moderation)

groups.title: Vector stores
groups.description:
Vector stores power semantic search for the Retrieval API and the `file_search` tool in the Responses and Assistants APIs.

Related guide: [File Search](/docs/assistants/tools/file-search)

groups.title: Vector store files
groups.description:
Vector store files represent files inside a vector store.

Related guide: [File Search](/docs/assistants/tools/file-search)

groups.title: Vector store file batches
groups.description:
Vector store file batches represent operations to add multiple files to a vector store.
Related guide: [File Search](/docs/assistants/tools/file-search)

groups.title: Assistants
groups.description:
Build assistants that can call models and use tools to perform tasks.

[Get started with the Assistants API](/docs/assistants)

groups.title: Threads
groups.description:
Create threads that assistants can interact with.

Related guide: [Assistants](/docs/assistants/overview)

groups.title: Messages
groups.description:
Create messages within threads

Related guide: [Assistants](/docs/assistants/overview)

groups.title: Runs
groups.description:
Represents an execution run on a thread.

Related guide: [Assistants](/docs/assistants/overview)

groups.title: Run steps
groups.description:
Represents the steps (model and tool calls) taken during the run.

Related guide: [Assistants](/docs/assistants/overview)

groups.title: Streaming
groups.description:
Stream the result of executing a Run or resuming a Run after submitting tool outputs.
You can stream events from the [Create Thread and Run](/docs/api-reference/runs/createThreadAndRun),
[Create Run](/docs/api-reference/runs/createRun), and [Submit Tool Outputs](/docs/api-reference/runs/submitToolOutputs)
endpoints by passing `"stream": true`. The response will be a [Server-Sent events](https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/server-sent-events.html#server-sent-events) stream.
Our Node and Python SDKs provide helpful utilities to make streaming easy. Reference the
[Assistants API quickstart](/docs/assistants/overview) to learn more.

groups.title: Invites
groups.description: Invite and manage invitations for an organization.

groups.title: Users
groups.description: Manage users and their role in an organization.

groups.title: Projects
groups.description:
Manage the projects within an orgnanization includes creation, updating, and archiving or projects.
The Default project cannot be archived.

groups.title: Project users
groups.description: Manage users within a project, including adding, updating roles, and removing users.

groups.title: Project service accounts
groups.description:
Manage service accounts within a project. A service account is a bot user that is not associated with a user.
If a user leaves an organization, their keys and membership in projects will no longer work. Service accounts
do not have this limitation. However, service accounts can also be deleted from a project.

groups.title: Project API keys
groups.description:
Manage API keys for a given project. Supports listing and deleting keys for users.
This API does not allow issuing keys for users, as users need to authorize themselves to generate keys.

groups.title: Project rate limits
groups.description: Manage rate limits per model for projects. Rate limits may be configured to be equal to or lower than the organization's rate limits.

groups.title: Audit logs
groups.description:
Logs of user actions and configuration changes within this organization.
To log events, you must activate logging in the [Organization Settings](/settings/organization/general).
Once activated, for security reasons, logging cannot be deactivated.

groups.title: Usage
groups.description:
The **Usage API** provides detailed insights into your activity across the OpenAI API. It also includes a separate [Costs endpoint](/docs/api-reference/usage/costs), which offers visibility into your spend, breaking down consumption by invoice line items and project IDs.

While the Usage API delivers granular usage data, it may not always reconcile perfectly with the Costs due to minor differences in how usage and spend are recorded. For financial purposes, we recommend using the [Costs endpoint](/docs/api-reference/usage/costs) or the [Costs tab](/settings/organization/usage) in the Usage Dashboard, which will reconcile back to your billing invoice.

groups.title: Certificates
groups.description:
Manage Mutual TLS certificates across your organization and projects.

[Learn more about Mutual TLS.](https://help.openai.com/en/articles/10876024-openai-mutual-tls-beta-program)

groups.title: Completions
groups.description: Given a prompt, the model will return one or more predicted completions along with the probabilities of alternative tokens at each position. Most developer should use our [Chat Completions API](/docs/guides/text-generation#text-generation-models) to leverage our best and newest models.
